{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UjeJiyvA1Pa4yRrbhYaye3","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Product Requirements Document\n     2→\n     3→## Project Name\n     4→\n     5→Bookmate\n     6→\n     7→## Overview\n     8→\n     9→Bookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n    10→\n    11→**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n    12→\n    13→## Goals\n    14→\n    15→- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n    16→- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n    17→- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n    18→\n    19→## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n    20→\n    21→### User Authentication\n    22→\n    23→Email + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n    24→\n    25→### Create a Reading Listing\n    26→\n    27→A user creates a listing with:\n    28→- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n    29→- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n    30→- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n    31→- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n    32→- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n    33→- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n    34→\n    35→### Browse Listings\n    36→\n    37→Simple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n    38→\n    39→### Join a Listing\n    40→\n    41→Any authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n    42→\n    43→### Group Formation\n    44→\n    45→When a listing reaches its max group size:\n    46→- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n    47→- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n    48→- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n    49→\n    50→### Notifications\n    51→\n    52→Email + in-app notifications for:\n    53→- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n    54→- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n    55→\n    56→### User Profile (Basic)\n    57→\n    58→- Display name\n    59→- Short bio\n    60→\n    61→## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n    62→\n    63→### Search and Filters\n    64→\n    65→- Search listings by book title or author\n    66→- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n    67→- Browsable feed with sorting options\n    68→\n    69→### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n    70→\n    71→Provide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n    72→\n    73→### Extended User Profile\n    74→\n    75→- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n    76→- \"Currently reading\" list\n    77→- Favorite genres\n    78→\n    79→## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n    80→\n    81→### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n    82→\n    83→System auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n    84→\n    85→### Applicant Approval System\n    86→\n    87→Listing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n    88→\n    89→### Rating and Reputation System\n    90→\n    91→Users can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n    92→\n    93→### Discord Support\n    94→\n    95→Allow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n    96→\n    97→## Non-Goals\n    98→\n    99→- No payment or monetization features\n   100→- No mobile app (web only)\n   101→- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n   102→- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n   103→- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n   104→- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n   105→\n   106→## Tech Stack\n   107→\n   108→- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n   109→- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n   110→- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n   111→- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n   112→- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n   113→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"f28a5171-b57c-4fb7-a99f-940ed5a50044","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:00:08.932Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/PRD.md","content":"# Product Requirements Document\n\n## Project Name\n\nBookmate\n\n## Overview\n\nBookmate is a web service that helps fiction readers find companions for reading and discussing books together. Users post listings for books they want to read, others join, and once a group forms, they connect via Telegram to coordinate their reading sessions. Think of it as matchmaking for book clubs.\n\n**Target audience:** English-speaking fiction readers who want structured reading companionship but struggle to find like-minded partners.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Make it effortless to find people who want to read the same book at the same pace\n- Remove the friction of organizing a reading group — from discovery to first meeting\n- Keep the platform lightweight: match readers, hand off to Telegram for discussion\n\n## P0 — Must Have (Core, launch blocker)\n\n### User Authentication\n\nEmail + password registration and login. Required to post or join any listing.\n\n### Create a Reading Listing\n\nA user creates a listing with:\n- **Book** — selected via Open Library API (title, author, cover art auto-populated)\n- **Preferred language** — language for discussion\n- **Reading pace** — e.g., \"1 chapter per week\", \"50 pages per day\"\n- **Start date** — when the group begins reading\n- **Meeting format** — voice calls, text chat, or mixed\n- **Max group size** — author sets the cap (e.g., 3–6 people)\n\n### Browse Listings\n\nSimple chronological list of open listings. No search or filters at this stage — just a scrollable feed of available reading groups to join.\n\n### Join a Listing\n\nAny authenticated user can join an open listing. First-come-first-served, no approval step. A user can be part of multiple reading groups simultaneously.\n\n### Group Formation\n\nWhen a listing reaches its max group size:\n- The listing becomes invisible to non-participants (effectively closed)\n- The author is prompted to share a Telegram group link with participants\n- The author manually creates the Telegram group and pastes the invite link\n\n### Notifications\n\nEmail + in-app notifications for:\n- **Each new sign-up** — listing author is notified when someone joins\n- **Group full** — all participants are notified when the group reaches its cap\n\n### User Profile (Basic)\n\n- Display name\n- Short bio\n\n## P1 — Should Have (Important, not launch blocker)\n\n### Search and Filters\n\n- Search listings by book title or author\n- Filter by reading pace, start date, meeting format\n- Browsable feed with sorting options\n\n### Telegram Group Creation Assistance\n\nProvide guidance or tooling to help the listing author create and share the Telegram group link more easily (e.g., step-by-step instructions, link validation).\n\n### Extended User Profile\n\n- Reading history (past groups participated in)\n- \"Currently reading\" list\n- Favorite genres\n\n## P2 — Nice to Have (Bonus, if time permits)\n\n### Automatic Telegram Group Creation\n\nSystem auto-creates a Telegram group via API and shares the invite link with all participants — no manual step needed.\n\n### Applicant Approval System\n\nListing author can review and approve/reject users who want to join, instead of first-come-first-served.\n\n### Rating and Reputation System\n\nUsers can rate their reading group experience. Build a reputation score based on participation history and peer ratings.\n\n### Discord Support\n\nAllow group creation via Discord as an alternative to Telegram.\n\n## Non-Goals\n\n- No payment or monetization features\n- No mobile app (web only)\n- No real-time chat within the platform (groups communicate via Telegram)\n- No complex third-party integrations beyond Open Library API\n- No AI-powered recommendations or matching (AI is allowed where it genuinely improves UX)\n- No content hosting (e-books, PDFs, etc.)\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n- **Frontend + Backend:** Next.js (full-stack)\n- **Database:** SQLite (local, persistent across container restarts)\n- **Analytics:** SQLite-based (no third-party analytics)\n- **Deployment:** Docker container with mounted volume for SQLite persistence\n- **Book data:** Open Library API (free, no API key required)\n","numLines":113,"startLine":1,"totalLines":113}}}

routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n    21→- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n    22→- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n    23→- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n    24→  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n    25→  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n    26→  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n    27→  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n    28→  - Skip-to-content link\n    29→  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n    30→  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n    31→  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n    32→\n    33→## Production\n    34→- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n    35→- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n    36→- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    37→- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n    38→- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    39→- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n    40→- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n    41→- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n    42→\n    43→## Open PRs\n    44→- None\n    45→\n    46→## Closed Recently\n    47→- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n    48→- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n    49→- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n    50→- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed\n    51→- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed\n    52→\n    53→## Open Issues\n    54→- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n    55→- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n    56→\n    57→## Important Notes\n    58→- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n    59→- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n    60→- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n    61→- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n    62→- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n    63→- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n    64→- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n    65→- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n    66→- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n    67→- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n    68→- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n    69→- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n    70→- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n    71→- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n    72→- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n    73→- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n    74→- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n    75→- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n    76→- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n    77→- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n    78→\n    79→## Test Suite\n    80→- 197 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n    81→- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n    82→- All API routes now have test coverage\n    83→- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n    84→\n    85→## Components\n    86→- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n    87→\n    88→## Next Session Priority\n    89→1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 days remaining, still needs-human\n    90→2. Agent Memory\n\n## Project\n- **Name**: Bookmate\n- **Repo**: https://github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate\n- **Project Board**: Bookmate (project #1 on TommyBrennan)\n- **Tech**: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, SQLite, iron-session\n- **Owner**: XanderZhu (human), TommyBrennan (GH account)\n\n## Current State\n- All PRD features implemented and merged (P0, P1, P2)\n- CI pipeline live: GitHub Actions runs lint + type check + build on push/PR to main\n- ESLint flat config with typescript-eslint, react-hooks, @next/eslint-plugin-next\n- Build passes clean, lint clean (no warnings)\n- Security hardening merged (PR #32, #36)\n- **Production deployed** via PM2 on port 3000 — http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- Health endpoint: `/api/health` (DB check, uptime, stats)\n- Custom 404 page (branded, with CTA)\n- OG + Twitter Card meta tags on listing pages (book cover, title, description)\n- Loading skeletons on all routes (root, listing detail, profile, notifications)\n- Error boundaries on all routes with retry functionality\n- Focus-visible styles for keyboard accessibility\n- **Accessibility improvements** (PR #49, merged + deployed):\n  - ARIA labels, roles, expanded states on all interactive elements\n  - Keyboard navigation on BookSearch dropdown (combobox pattern)\n  - autocomplete attributes on auth forms\n  - role=\"alert\" on error messages\n  - Skip-to-content link\n  - role=\"tablist\"/tab/tabpanel on profile tabs\n  - role=\"radiogroup\"/radio on format/platform buttons\n  - role=\"checkbox\" with keyboard support on approval toggle\n\n## Production\n- **URL**: http://89.167.127.85:3000\n- **Process manager**: PM2 (via npx)\n- **Config**: `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- **Deploy script**: `scripts/deploy.sh`\n- **Logs**: `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n- **No reverse proxy** (nginx requires root — not available)\n- **No HTTPS** (no domain configured, no certbot)\n- PM2 process saved: `npx pm2 save` (restores on `pm2 resurrect`)\n\n## Open PRs\n- None\n\n## Closed Recently\n- #91: Code review round 8 — timing-safe secrets, type validation, stale transaction data, email HTML escaping — merged + deployed\n- #87: Code review round 6 — logout fix, is_full reset, email subjects, webhook validation, fetch abort — merged + deployed\n- #85: Test coverage for Telegram and Discord API routes (69 new tests) — merged\n- #83: Code review round 5 — duplicate notifications, platform link timing, validation — merged + deployed\n- #81: Code review round 4 — email normalization, application state, rate limiter leak — merged + deployed\n\n## Open Issues\n- #21: Telegram bot token (needs-human) — multiple follow-up reminders sent, still waiting\n- #54: **PAT expires ~March 27** (needs-human) — GH_TOKEN renewal required, ~3 days left\n\n## Important Notes\n- GH_TOKEN loaded from `.claude-beat/.env`\n- Remote URL includes PAT: reset with `git remote set-url` if needed\n- Next.js 15 used (not 16) due to Turbopack /root permission issue\n- agent-browser WORKS if: export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"\n- agent-browser crashes after extended use (memory pressure) — keep recording sessions short, restart between scenes\n- ffmpeg installed as static binary in ~/bin — add to PATH: export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"\n- ELEVENLABS_API_KEY not available — demo video has no voiceover\n- Demo video at .claude-beat/logs/demos/demo_2026-03-20.mp4 (5 scenes, ~1:51)\n- SQLite DB stored in `app/data/` (gitignored)\n- DB tables: users, listings, listing_members, listing_applications, user_genres, notifications, ratings, telegram_chats, pending_telegram_groups\n- Telegram bot integration requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN env var (see issue #21)\n- Discord bot integration requires DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN + DISCORD_CLIENT_ID + DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET env vars\n- Listings have `platform_preference` field (telegram/discord)\n- Email notifications require SMTP env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_FROM, etc.)\n- nodemailer added as dependency for email sending\n- Session secret: lazy runtime check (not module-level) to avoid build failure\n- ESLint: use eslint v9 + @eslint/js v9 (not v10) to avoid peer dep conflicts\n- npm ci works cleanly (no --legacy-peer-deps needed)\n- No root/sudo access on server — can't install nginx, Docker, or system packages\n- PM2 available via npx (not globally installed)\n\n## Test Suite\n- 197 tests across 22 test files, all passing\n- Tested: auth (login, register), health, listings (browse, detail, create, join, edit, delete, telegram, discord, auto-telegram, auto-discord), notifications, profile, profile/genres, profile/reading, profile/reputation, ratings, rate-limit, telegram/setup, telegram/webhook, discord/setup, discord/webhook\n- All API routes now have test coverage\n- @vitest/coverage-v8 installed for coverage reporting\n\n## Components\n- `BookCover` — reusable book cover component with error fallback, uses `unoptimized` to bypass Next.js image proxy for Open Library covers that redirect through archive.org\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. **URGENT**: PAT expires ~March 27 (#54) — ~3 {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012paoogthtdTDoYtM3Mx2Jq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Learned Patterns\n     2→\n     3→## GitHub Authentication\n     4→- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n     5→  ```bash\n     6→  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n     7→  ```\n     8→- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n     9→- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n    10→\n    11→## Session Flow\n    12→- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n    13→- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n    14→- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n    15→\n    16→## Environment\n    17→- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n    18→- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n    19→- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n    20→- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n    21→- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n    22→- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n    23→\n    24→## Build\n    25→- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n    26→- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n    27→- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n    28→- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n    29→- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n    30→\n    31→## Browser Testing\n    32→- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n    33→- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n    34→- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n    35→- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n    36→- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n    37→- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n    38→- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n    39→- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n    40→- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n    41→\n    42→## Deployment\n    43→- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n    44→- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n    45→- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n    46→- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n    47→- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n    48→- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n    49→- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n    50→- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n    51→\n    52→## React + SQLite\n    53→- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n    54→- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n    55→\n    56→## Security\n    57→- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n    58→- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n    59→- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n    60→- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n    61→- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n    62→\n    63→## Working Directory\n    64→- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n    65→- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n    66→- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n    67→\n    68→## Security\n    69→- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n    70→- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n    71→- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n    72→- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n    73→- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n    74→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"b579f222-2fbc-420d-913a-13a4a9f7cb5c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:00:15.584Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/memory/patterns.md","content":"# Learned Patterns\n\n## GitHub Authentication\n- Always export GH_TOKEN from .env before running gh commands:\n  ```bash\n  export GH_TOKEN=$(grep GH_TOKEN /root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/.env | cut -d= -f2)\n  ```\n- Cannot `--add-reviewer` when token owner is the PR author (HTTP 422)\n- Repo created under TommyBrennan (not XanderZhu — can't create for other users)\n\n## Session Flow\n- First session bootstraps repo, creates issues from PRD as `approved` + `prd`\n- PRD features are pre-approved — no proposal step needed\n- Focus on housekeeping when blocked: organize project board, update memory\n\n## Environment\n- Find agent-browser with: `which agent-browser`\n- agent-browser requires `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp` prefix due to socket dir permissions\n- Playwright chromium needs manual install: `npx playwright install chromium`\n- Git remote uses PAT in URL: `https://ghp_...@github.com/TommyBrennan/bookmate.git`\n- Git remote URL may lose PAT between sessions — re-set with `git remote set-url`\n- Must set `git config user.email` and `user.name` each session if not set globally\n\n## Build\n- Next.js 16 Turbopack fails in this environment (can't read /root dir)\n- Use Next.js 15 which defaults to webpack\n- Build from app/ directory: `cd app && npx next build`\n- `useRef<T>()` needs explicit `undefined` arg in React 19: `useRef<T>(undefined)`\n- better-sqlite3 `.all()` returns `unknown[]` — cast explicitly for TS\n\n## Browser Testing\n- agent-browser WORKS with: `export XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/tmp/runtime-$(id -u) && mkdir -p \"$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR\"`\n- Crashes after extended use (~5+ min) due to Chromium memory pressure\n- Restart between recording scenes: `agent-browser close && rm -rf $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/agent-browser/ && sleep 2`\n- For snapshot timeouts on pages with many elements (e.g., book search dropdown), use `agent-browser screenshot` or JS eval instead\n- Book search results can't use `find text` (multiple matches) — use JS: `document.querySelector(\"button[type=button].w-full\").click()`\n- Reading pace input ref (@e17) times out — use `agent-browser eval` with nativeInputValueSetter to set values\n- For API testing: `curl -s URL | python3 -m json.tool`\n- ffmpeg: static binary at ~/bin/ffmpeg — `export PATH=\"$HOME/bin:$PATH\"` needed each session\n- When recording demos: login user BEFORE starting recording, then navigate to target page\n\n## Deployment\n- No root/sudo access — can't apt-get install, create systemd services, or install nginx\n- PM2 available via npx (not global): `npx pm2 start/stop/restart/list/logs`\n- Deploy script: `./scripts/deploy.sh` (pull, install, build, restart PM2)\n- Production runs on port 3000 directly (no reverse proxy)\n- Public IP: 89.167.127.85 (IPv4), 2a01:4f9:c013:600f::1 (IPv6)\n- Kill stale dev servers before deploy: check `ss -tlnp | grep 3000`\n- PM2 ecosystem config at `app/ecosystem.config.js`\n- PM2 logs at `.claude-beat/logs/pm2-{out,error}.log`\n\n## React + SQLite\n- SQLite stores booleans as integers (0/1). In JSX, `{0 && <Component />}` renders \"0\" as text. Always use `!!value` or `Boolean(value)` for numeric fields in `&&` conditionals.\n- Git `add` with bracket directories like `[id]` needs escaping or use from project root\n\n## Security\n- Telegram webhook commands (/link, /link_ID) must verify the sender is the listing author via telegram_user_links table\n- Always validate external URLs before storing (e.g., book_cover_url must start with https://covers.openlibrary.org/)\n- Notification failures should never mask successful DB transactions — wrap in separate try/catch\n- Move related DB writes inside the same transaction for atomicity (e.g., rejection of pending apps when group fills)\n- Query string parameters from URL are always strings — parseInt() before DB queries on integer columns\n\n## Working Directory\n- Project root: /root/Projects/bookmate\n- App directory: /root/Projects/bookmate/app\n- Always use full paths for .claude-beat/.env references\n\n## Security\n- Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for secret comparisons (not `!==`) to prevent timing oracle attacks\n- Always `typeof` check JSON body fields before string operations — `parseInt(obj)` silently coerces objects via `.toString()`\n- HTML-escape user-controlled content before embedding in email HTML body\n- Re-read mutable DB values inside transactions to prevent stale data from concurrent writes\n- `lib/crypto-utils.ts` provides `safeCompare()` and `escapeHtmlForEmail()` utilities\n","numLines":74,"startLine":1,"totalLines":74}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SXjhHPqt9Ws4MeHmivLgiQ","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-24 00:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 7: found and fixed 4 security/correctness bugs. Created issue #88, implemented fixes in PR #89, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    12→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #88: Code review round 7 (PR #89 — merged + deployed)\n    17→\n    18→4 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **HTML injection in Telegram messages** (CRITICAL) — Book titles were interpolated directly into HTML-mode Telegram messages (`<b>${bookTitle}</b>`). Added `escapeHtml()` helper to sanitize `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"` characters. Applied to all user content in Telegram webhook route.\n    21→\n    22→2. **Telegram /start auth bypass** (IMPORTANT) — `/start listing_ID` command linked any group to any listing without checking ownership. Now verifies the sender is the listing author via `telegram_user_links` table. Non-authors get a message to use `/link` instead.\n    23→\n    24→3. **Discord guildId/channelId path traversal** (IMPORTANT) — `guildId` and `channelId` from webhook payload were used unvalidated in Discord API URL paths (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). Added `isValidSnowflake()` validation (17-20 digit numeric strings).\n    25→\n    26→4. **Missing discord_link in profile reading** (IMPORTANT) — Profile reading API returned `telegram_link` but not `discord_link`. Added to SQL query and TypeScript interface.\n    27→\n    28→All 198 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    29→\n    30→## Issues Monitored\n    31→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n    32→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    33→\n    34→## Next Session Priority\n    35→1. Check production health\n    36→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    37→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    38→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"2c9f9939-cbc7-4acd-9ce5-9c87c0acb069","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:00:16.012Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-24-0000.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-24 00:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 7: found and fixed 4 security/correctness bugs. Created issue #88, implemented fixes in PR #89, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online, 198 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: GitHub notifications for #74 (already resolved), NomadLingo (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #88: Code review round 7 (PR #89 — merged + deployed)\n\n4 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **HTML injection in Telegram messages** (CRITICAL) — Book titles were interpolated directly into HTML-mode Telegram messages (`<b>${bookTitle}</b>`). Added `escapeHtml()` helper to sanitize `<`, `>`, `&`, `\"` characters. Applied to all user content in Telegram webhook route.\n\n2. **Telegram /start auth bypass** (IMPORTANT) — `/start listing_ID` command linked any group to any listing without checking ownership. Now verifies the sender is the listing author via `telegram_user_links` table. Non-authors get a message to use `/link` instead.\n\n3. **Discord guildId/channelId path traversal** (IMPORTANT) — `guildId` and `channelId` from webhook payload were used unvalidated in Discord API URL paths (`/guilds/${guildId}/channels`). Added `isValidSnowflake()` validation (17-20 digit numeric strings).\n\n4. **Missing discord_link in profile reading** (IMPORTANT) — Profile reading API returned `telegram_link` but not `discord_link`. Added to SQL query and TypeScript interface.\n\nAll 198 tests pass (1 new), ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":38,"startLine":1,"totalLines":38}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HUFWXmDShUv1WW1jZczW76","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Session Log — 2026-03-23 23:00 UTC\n     2→\n     3→## Summary\n     4→Code review round 6: found and fixed 5 bugs including a critical logout failure. Created issue #86, implemented fixes in PR #87, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n     5→\n     6→## Review Findings\n     7→- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (112min uptime), 197 tests pass\n     8→- **CI**: Green on main\n     9→- **Open PRs**: None\n    10→- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n    11→- **Emails**: Issue #74 notifications (already closed), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n    12→- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n    13→\n    14→## Work Completed\n    15→\n    16→### Issue #86: Code review round 6 (PR #87 — merged + deployed)\n    17→\n    18→5 bugs fixed:\n    19→\n    20→1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (CRITICAL) — In `logout/route.ts` and `me/route.ts`, `session.destroy()` is async but was called without `await`. The cookie was never cleared, so users remained logged in until the 7-day session expiry. Fixed by adding `await`.\n    21→\n    22→2. **`is_full` not reset on group resize** (CRITICAL) — When an author increased `maxGroupSize` on a previously-full listing, `is_full` stayed `1` and the listing remained permanently invisible in browse. Fixed by checking member count vs new size in the PATCH handler and resetting `is_full = 0` when appropriate.\n    23→\n    24→3. **Missing email subject cases** (IMPORTANT) — `telegram_ready` and `discord_ready` notification types fell through to generic \"Bookmate: Notification\" subject. Added proper subject lines.\n    25→\n    26→4. **Discord webhook `listingId` validation** (IMPORTANT) — `handleLink` accepted non-integer/negative `listingId` values. Added integer validation consistent with other routes.\n    27→\n    28→5. **BookSearch fetch race condition** (IMPORTANT) — Added `AbortController` to cancel in-flight Open Library API requests on component unmount or rapid query changes, preventing stale responses from overwriting newer results.\n    29→\n    30→All existing 197 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n    31→\n    32→## Issues Monitored\n    33→- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n    34→- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n    35→\n    36→## Next Session Priority\n    37→1. Check production health\n    38→2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n    39→3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n    40→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"8a1b85d7-768a-4ff4-bf3d-3ab1345d2ff4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:00:16.557Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/root/Projects/bookmate/.claude-beat/logs/session-2026-03-23-2300.md","content":"# Session Log — 2026-03-23 23:00 UTC\n\n## Summary\nCode review round 6: found and fixed 5 bugs including a critical logout failure. Created issue #86, implemented fixes in PR #87, CI passed, merged to main, deployed to production.\n\n## Review Findings\n- **Production**: Healthy — HTTP 200, PM2 online (112min uptime), 197 tests pass\n- **CI**: Green on main\n- **Open PRs**: None\n- **Open issues**: #54 (needs-human, PAT expiry ~3 days), #21 (needs-human, Telegram token)\n- **Emails**: Issue #74 notifications (already closed), NomadLingo issues (different project), Google Workspace promo\n- **PRD sync**: All PRD features have corresponding closed issues\n\n## Work Completed\n\n### Issue #86: Code review round 6 (PR #87 — merged + deployed)\n\n5 bugs fixed:\n\n1. **`session.destroy()` not awaited** (CRITICAL) — In `logout/route.ts` and `me/route.ts`, `session.destroy()` is async but was called without `await`. The cookie was never cleared, so users remained logged in until the 7-day session expiry. Fixed by adding `await`.\n\n2. **`is_full` not reset on group resize** (CRITICAL) — When an author increased `maxGroupSize` on a previously-full listing, `is_full` stayed `1` and the listing remained permanently invisible in browse. Fixed by checking member count vs new size in the PATCH handler and resetting `is_full = 0` when appropriate.\n\n3. **Missing email subject cases** (IMPORTANT) — `telegram_ready` and `discord_ready` notification types fell through to generic \"Bookmate: Notification\" subject. Added proper subject lines.\n\n4. **Discord webhook `listingId` validation** (IMPORTANT) — `handleLink` accepted non-integer/negative `listingId` values. Added integer validation consistent with other routes.\n\n5. **BookSearch fetch race condition** (IMPORTANT) — Added `AbortController` to cancel in-flight Open Library API requests on component unmount or rapid query changes, preventing stale responses from overwriting newer results.\n\nAll existing 197 tests pass, ESLint clean, build clean, CI green.\n\n## Issues Monitored\n- **#54 (PAT expiry)**: ~3 days remaining — critical, needs-human\n- **#21 (Telegram token)**: Still needs-human\n\n## Next Session Priority\n1. Check production health\n2. Monitor PAT expiry (#54) — critical\n3. Consider E2E testing, performance improvements, or new feature proposals\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XJQSiEJ5dXs1mTwJvLttWR","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     5→\n     6→interface BookResult {\n     7→  key: string;\n     8→  title: string;\n     9→  author_name?: string[];\n    10→  cover_i?: number;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→interface Props {\n    14→  onSelect: (book: {\n    15→    title: string;\n    16→    author: string;\n    17→    coverUrl: string;\n    18→    olid: string;\n    19→  }) => void;\n    20→}\n    21→\n    22→export default function BookSearch({ onSelect }: Props) {\n    23→  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    24→  const [results, setResults] = useState<BookResult[]>([]);\n    25→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    26→  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);\n    27→  const [activeIndex, setActiveIndex] = useState(-1);\n    28→  const timeoutRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    29→  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController>(undefined);\n    30→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    31→  const listboxRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    32→\n    33→  useEffect(() => {\n    34→    const handler = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    35→      if (\n    36→        containerRef.current &&\n    37→        !containerRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)\n    38→      ) {\n    39→        setOpen(false);\n    40→      }\n    41→    };\n    42→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    43→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handler);\n    44→  }, []);\n    45→\n    46→  useEffect(() => {\n    47→    if (query.length < 3) {\n    48→      setResults([]);\n    49→      return;\n    50→    }\n    51→\n    52→    if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    53→    if (abortRef.current) abortRef.current.abort();\n    54→\n    55→    const controller = new AbortController();\n    56→    abortRef.current = controller;\n    57→\n    58→    timeoutRef.current = setTimeout(async () => {\n    59→      setLoading(true);\n    60→      try {\n    61→        const res = await fetch(\n    62→          `https://openlibrary.org/search.json?q=${encodeURIComponent(query)}&limit=8&fields=key,title,author_name,cover_i`,\n    63→          { signal: controller.signal }\n    64→        );\n    65→        const data = await res.json();\n    66→        setResults(data.docs || []);\n    67→        setOpen(true);\n    68→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n    69→      } catch (err) {\n    70→        if ((err as Error).name !== \"AbortError\") {\n    71→          setResults([]);\n    72→        }\n    73→      } finally {\n    74→        if (!controller.signal.aborted) {\n    75→          setLoading(false);\n    76→        }\n    77→      }\n    78→    }, 400);\n    79→\n    80→    return () => {\n    81→      if (timeoutRef.current) clearTimeout(timeoutRef.current);\n    82→      controller.abort();\n    83→    };\n    84→  }, [query]);\n    85→\n    86→  const handleSelect = (book: BookResult) => {\n    87→    const olid = book.key.replace(\"/works/\", \"\");\n    88→    onSelect({\n    89→      title: book.title,\n    90→      author: book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\",\n    91→      coverUrl: book.cover_i\n    92→        ? `https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-M.jpg`\n    93→        : \"\",\n    94→      olid,\n    95→    });\n    96→    setQuery(book.title);\n    97→    setOpen(false);\n    98→    setActiveIndex(-1);\n    99→  };\n   100→\n   101→  const handleKeyDown = (e: React.KeyboardEvent) => {\n   102→    if (!open || results.length === 0) return;\n   103→\n   104→    switch (e.key) {\n   105→      case \"ArrowDown\":\n   106→        e.preventDefault();\n   107→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev < results.length - 1 ? prev + 1 : 0));\n   108→        break;\n   109→      case \"ArrowUp\":\n   110→        e.preventDefault();\n   111→        setActiveIndex((prev) => (prev > 0 ? prev - 1 : results.length - 1));\n   112→        break;\n   113→      case \"Enter\":\n   114→        e.preventDefault();\n   115→        if (activeIndex >= 0 && activeIndex < results.length) {\n   116→          handleSelect(results[activeIndex]);\n   117→        }\n   118→        break;\n   119→      case \"Escape\":\n   120→        setOpen(false);\n   121→        setActiveIndex(-1);\n   122→        break;\n   123→    }\n   124→  };\n   125→\n   126→  // Scroll active option into view\n   127→  useEffect(() => {\n   128→    if (activeIndex >= 0 && listboxRef.current) {\n   129→      const activeEl = listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex] as HTMLElement;\n   130→      if (activeEl) {\n   131→        activeEl.scrollIntoView({ block: \"nearest\" });\n   132→      }\n   133→    }\n   134→  }, [activeIndex]);\n   135→\n   136→  const activeDescendant =\n   137→    activeIndex >= 0 && results[activeIndex]\n   138→      ? `book-option-${results[activeIndex].key}`\n   139→      : undefined;\n   140→\n   141→  return (\n   142→    <div ref={containerRef} className=\"relative\">\n   143→      <input\n   144→        type=\"text\"\n   145→        className=\"input-field\"\n   146→        value={query}\n   147→        onChange={(e) => setQuery(e.target.value)}\n   148→        onKeyDown={handleKeyDown}\n   149→        placeholder=\"Search for a book title...\"\n   150→        onFocus={() => results.length > 0 && setOpen(true)}\n   151→        role=\"combobox\"\n   152→        aria-expanded={open && results.length > 0}\n   153→        aria-haspopup=\"listbox\"\n   154→        aria-controls=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   155→        aria-activedescendant={activeDescendant}\n   156→        aria-label=\"Search for a book\"\n   157→        aria-autocomplete=\"list\"\n   158→      />\n   159→      {loading && (\n   160→        <div\n   161→          className=\"absolute right-3 top-3 text-xs\"\n   162→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   163→          role=\"status\"\n   164→          aria-live=\"polite\"\n   165→        >\n   166→          Searching...\n   167→        </div>\n   168→      )}\n   169→\n   170→      {open && results.length > 0 && (\n   171→        <div\n   172→          ref={listboxRef}\n   173→          id=\"book-search-listbox\"\n   174→          role=\"listbox\"\n   175→          aria-label=\"Book search results\"\n   176→          className=\"absolute z-10 w-full mt-1 card shadow-lg max-h-72 overflow-y-auto\"\n   177→          style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 0\" }}\n   178→        >\n   179→          {results.map((book, index) => (\n   180→            <button\n   181→              key={book.key}\n   182→              id={`book-option-${book.key}`}\n   183→              type=\"button\"\n   184→              role=\"option\"\n   185→              aria-selected={index === activeIndex}\n   186→              onClick={() => handleSelect(book)}\n   187→              className=\"w-full text-left px-4 py-2 hover:bg-gray-50 flex items-center gap-3 cursor-pointer\"\n   188→              style={{\n   189→                backgroundColor: index === activeIndex ? \"rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.04)\" : undefined,\n   190→              }}\n   191→            >\n   192→              {book.cover_i ? (\n   193→                <Image\n   194→                  src={`https://covers.openlibrary.org/b/id/${book.cover_i}-S.jpg`}\n   195→                  alt={`Cover of ${book.title}`}\n   196→                  width={32}\n   197→                  height={48}\n   198→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 object-cover rounded\"\n   199→                />\n   200→              ) : (\n   201→                <div\n   202→                  className=\"w-8 h-12 rounded flex items-center justify-center text-sm\"\n   203→                  style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\" }}\n   204→                  aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   205→                >\n   206→                  <span role=\"img\" aria-hidden=\"true\">📖</span>\n   207→                </div>\n   208→              )}\n   209→              <div className=\"min-w-0\">\n   210→                <div\n   211→                  className=\"text-sm font-semibold truncate\"\n   212→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   213→                >\n   214→                  {book.title}\n   215→                </div>\n   216→                <div\n   217→                  className=\"text-xs truncate\"\n   218→                  style={{\n   219→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   220→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   221→                  }}\n   222→                >\n   223→                  {book.author_name?.[0] || \"Unknown author\"}\n   224→                </div>\n   225→              </div>\n   226→            </button>\n   227→          ))}\n   228→        </div>\n   229→      )}\n   230→    </div>\n   231→  );\n   232→}\n   233→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01D692zmnNcsB7Rqcev8ZVMP","session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"8664c4c3-68cb-4314-b7b9-fef44b35a333","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:01:19.672Z"}

\"next/link\";\n     4→import { useEffect, useState, useRef } from \"react\";\n     5→import { useRouter, usePathname } from \"next/navigation\";\n     6→\n     7→interface User {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  email: string;\n    10→  displayName: string;\n    11→}\n    12→\n    13→export default function Navbar() {\n    14→  const [user, setUser] = useState<User | null>(null);\n    15→  const [notifCount, setNotifCount] = useState(0);\n    16→  const [menuOpen, setMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    17→  const [mobileMenuOpen, setMobileMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n    18→  const router = useRouter();\n    19→  const pathname = usePathname();\n    20→  const menuRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n    21→\n    22→  useEffect(() => {\n    23→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    24→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    25→      .then((data) => {\n    26→        if (data.user) {\n    27→          setUser(data.user);\n    28→          setNotifCount(data.user.unreadNotificationCount || 0);\n    29→        }\n    30→      })\n    31→      .catch(() => {\n    32→        // Auth check failed — stay logged out\n    33→      });\n    34→  }, []);\n    35→\n    36→  // Close menus on outside click\n    37→  useEffect(() => {\n    38→    const handleClickOutside = (e: MouseEvent) => {\n    39→      if (menuRef.current && !menuRef.current.contains(e.target as Node)) {\n    40→        setMenuOpen(false);\n    41→      }\n    42→    };\n    43→    document.addEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    44→    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"mousedown\", handleClickOutside);\n    45→  }, []);\n    46→\n    47→  // Close mobile menu on route change\n    48→  useEffect(() => {\n    49→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    50→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    51→  }, [pathname]);\n    52→\n    53→  const handleLogout = async () => {\n    54→    await fetch(\"/api/auth/logout\", { method: \"POST\" });\n    55→    setUser(null);\n    56→    setMenuOpen(false);\n    57→    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n    58→    router.push(\"/\");\n    59→    router.refresh();\n    60→  };\n    61→\n    62→  return (\n    63→    <nav\n    64→      className=\"border-b sticky top-0 z-50\"\n    65→      style={{\n    66→        borderColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n    67→        backgroundColor: \"var(--color-bg)\",\n    68→      }}\n    69→    >\n    70→      <div className=\"max-w-5xl mx-auto px-3 sm:px-4 h-14 sm:h-16 flex items-center justify-between\">\n    71→        <Link\n    72→          href=\"/\"\n    73→          className=\"text-lg sm:text-xl font-bold\"\n    74→          style={{ color: \"var(--color-accent)\", letterSpacing: \"-0.03em\" }}\n    75→          onClick={() => setMobileMenuOpen(false)}\n    76→        >\n    77→          bookmate\n    78→        </Link>\n    79→\n    80→        {/* Desktop nav */}\n    81→        <div className=\"hidden sm:flex items-center gap-4\">\n    82→          {user ? (\n    83→            <>\n    84→              <Link\n    85→                href=\"/listings/create\"\n    86→                className=\"btn-primary text-sm\"\n    87→                style={{ padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\" }}\n    88→              >\n    89→                + New Listing\n    90→              </Link>\n    91→\n    92→              <div className=\"relative\" ref={menuRef}>\n    93→                <button\n    94→                  onClick={() => setMenuOpen(!menuOpen)}\n    95→                  className=\"flex items-center gap-2 cursor-pointer\"\n    96→                  style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif\" }}\n    97→                  aria-label={`User menu for ${user.displayName}${notifCount > 0 ? `, ${notifCount} unread notifications` : \"\"}`}\n    98→                  aria-expanded={menuOpen}\n    99→                  aria-haspopup=\"true\"\n   100→                >\n   101→                  <div\n   102→                    className=\"w-8 h-8 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-bold text-white\"\n   103→                    style={{ backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\" }}\n   104→                  >\n   105→                    {user.displayName.charAt(0).toUpperCase()}\n   106→                  </div>\n   107→                  {notifCount > 0 && (\n   108→                    <span\n   109→ src,\n    18→  alt,\n    19→  width,\n    20→  height,\n    21→  className = \"\",\n    22→  fallbackClassName = \"\",\n    23→  style,\n    24→}: BookCoverProps) {\n    25→  const [hasError, setHasError] = useState(false);\n    26→\n    27→  if (!src || hasError) {\n    28→    return (\n    29→      <div\n    30→        className={\n    31→          fallbackClassName ||\n    32→          `${className} flex items-center justify-center text-2xl`\n    33→        }\n    34→        style={{\n    35→          width,\n    36→          height,\n    37→          backgroundColor: \"var(--color-border)\",\n    38→          flexShrink: 0,\n    39→          color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    40→          ...style,\n    41→        }}\n    42→        role=\"img\"\n    43→        aria-label={`Cover for ${alt}`}\n    44→      >\n    45→        📖\n    46→      </div>\n    47→    );\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  return (\n    51→    <Image\n    52→      src={src}\n    53→      alt={alt}\n    54→      width={width}\n    55→      height={height}\n    56→      className={className}\n    57→      style={style}\n    58→      unoptimized\n    59→      onError={() => setHasError(true)}\n    60→    />\n    61→  );\n    62→}\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LCP4szjcgejeEux7qWfdhf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import BookCover from \"@/components/BookCover\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→\n    44→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    45→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    46→      setLoading(true);\n    47→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    48→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    49→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    50→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    51→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    52→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    53→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    54→\n    55→      const qs = params.toString();\n    56→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    57→        .then((r) => {\n    58→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    59→          return r.json();\n    60→        })\n    61→        .then((data) => {\n    62→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    63→          if (data.pagination) {\n    64→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    65→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    66→          }\n    67→          setError(null);\n    68→          setLoading(false);\n    69→        })\n    70→        .catch((err) => {\n    71→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    72→          setLoading(false);\n    73→        });\n    74→    },\n    75→    []\n    76→  );\n    77→\n    78→  // Initial fetch\n    79→  useEffect(() => {\n    80→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    81→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    82→\n    83→  // Debounced search\n    84→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    86→    setPage(1);\n    87→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    88→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    89→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    90→    }, 400);\n    91→  };\n    92→\n    93→  // Immediate filter changes\n    94→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    95→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    96→    setPage(1);\n    97→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n   101→    setReadingPace(value);\n   102→    setPage(1);\n   103→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n   104→  };\n   105→\n   106→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n   107→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n   108→    setPage(1);\n   109→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n   110→  };\n   111→\n   112→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n   113→    setSort(value);\n   114→    setPage(1);\n   115→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   116→  };\n   117→\n   118→  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n   119→    setPage(newPage);\n   120→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n   121→    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n   122→  };\n   123→\n   124→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   125→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   126→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   127→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   128→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   129→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   130→    setPage(1);\n   131→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n   132→  };\n   133→\n   134→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   135→\n   136→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   137→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   138→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   139→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   140→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   141→    };\n   142→    return labels[f] || f;\n   143→  };\n   144→\n   145→  return (\n   146→    <div>\n   147→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   148→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   149→          Find your reading companion\n   150→        </h1>\n   151→        <p\n   152→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   153→          style={{\n   154→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   155→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   156→          }}\n   157→        >\n   158→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   159→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   160→        </p>\n   161→      </div>\n   162→\n   163→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   164→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   165→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n   166→          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n   167→            <input\n   168→              type=\"text\"\n   169→              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n   170→              value={searchQuery}\n   171→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   172→              className=\"input-field\"\n   173→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   174→            />\n   175→            <svg\n   176→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   177→              style={{\n   178→                position: \"absolute\",\n   179→                left: \"0.75rem\",\n   180→                top: \"50%\",\n   181→                transform: \"translateY(-50%)\",\n   182→                color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   183→                width: \"1.125rem\",\n   184→                height: \"1.125rem\",\n   185→                pointerEvents: \"none\",\n   186→              }}\n   187→              fill=\"none\"\n   188→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   189→              strokeWidth={2}\n   190→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   191→            >\n   192→              <circle cx={11} cy={11} r={8} />\n   193→              <line x1={21} y1={21} x2={16.65} y2={16.65} />\n   194→            </svg>\n   195→          </div>\n   196→          <button\n   197→            onClick={() => setFiltersOpen(!filtersOpen)}\n   198→            className=\"btn-secondary\"\n   199→            aria-expanded={filtersOpen}\n   200→            aria-controls=\"filter-panel\"\n   201→            style={{\n   202→              display: \"flex\",\n   203→              alignItems: \"center\",\n   204→              gap: \"0.375rem\",\n   205→              whiteSpace: \"nowrap\",\n   206→              position: \"relative\",\n   207→            }}\n   208→          >\n   209→            <svg\n   210→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   211→              width=\"16\"\n   212→              height=\"16\"\n   213→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   214→              fill=\"none\"\n   215→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   216→              strokeWidth={2}\n   217→            >\n   218→              <line x1={4} y1={6} x2={20} y2={6} />\n   219→              <line x1={7} y1={12} x2={17} y2={12} />\n   220→              <line x1={10} y1={18} x2={14} y2={18} />\n   221→            </svg>\n   222→            Filters\n   223→            {hasActiveFilters && (\n   224→              <span\n   225→                aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   226→                style={{\n   227→                  width: \"0.5rem\",\n   228→                  height: \"0.5rem\",\n   229→                  borderRadius: \"50%\",\n   230→                  backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n   231→                  position: \"absolute\",\n   232→                  top: \"0.375rem\",\n   233→                  right: \"0.375rem\",\n   234→                }}\n   235→              />\n   236→            )}\n   237→          </button>\n   238→        </div>\n   239→\n   240→        {/* Filter panel */}\n   241→        {filtersOpen && (\n   242→          <div\n   243→            id=\"filter-panel\"\n   244→            className=\"card mt-3\"\n   245→            style={{ padding: \"1rem 1.25rem\" }}\n   246→          >\n   247→            <div\n   248→              className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3 sm:gap-4 items-end\"\n   249→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   250→            >\n   251→              <div>\n   252→                <label\n   253→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   254→                  style={{\n   255→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   256→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   257→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   258→                  }}\n   259→                >\n   260→                  Meeting format\n   261→                </label>\n   262→                <select\n   263→                  value={meetingFormat}\n   264→                  onChange={(e) => handleFormatChange(e.target.value)}\n   265→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   266→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   267→                >\n   268→                  <option value=\"\">All formats</option>\n   269→                  <option value=\"voice\">Voice calls</option>\n   270→                  <option value=\"text\">Text chat</option>\n   271→                  <option value=\"mixed\">Mixed</option>\n   272→                </select>\n   273→              </div>\n   274→\n   275→              <div>\n   276→                <label\n   277→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   278→                  style={{\n   279→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   280→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   281→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   282→                  }}\n   283→                >\n   284→                  Reading pace\n   285→                </label>\n   286→                <select\n   287→                  value={readingPace}\n   288→                  onChange={(e) => handlePaceChange(e.target.value)}\n   289→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   290→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   291→                >\n   292→                  <option value=\"\">All paces</option>\n   293→                  <option value=\"chapter\">Chapter-based</option>\n   294→                  <option value=\"pages\">Pages-based</option>\n   295→                  <option value=\"week\">Weekly</option>\n   296→                  <option value=\"day\">Daily</option>\n   297→                </select>\n   298→              </div>\n   299→\n   300→              <div>\n >\n   309→                  Starting from\n   310→                </label>\n   311→                <input\n   312→                  type=\"date\"\n   313→                  value={startDateFrom}\n padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   317→                />\n   318→              </div>\n   319→\n   320→              <div>\n   321→                <label\n   322→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   323→                  style={{\n   324→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   325→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   326→ )}\n   363→      </div>\n   364→\n   365→      {/* Results */}\n   366→      {loading ? (\n   367→        <div className=\"grid gap-4\">\n   368→          {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (\n   369→            <div key={i} className=\"card flex gap-4\">\n   370→              <div\n   371→                className=\"skeleton\"\n   372→                style={{ width: \"4rem\", height: \"6rem\", flexShrink: 0 }}\n   373→              />\n   374→              <div className=\"flex-1\">\n   375→                <div\n   376→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\"\n   377→                  style={{ width: `${60 - i * 5}%` }}\n   378→                />\n   379→                <div\n   380→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-text\"\n   381→                  style={{ width: `${45 - i * 5}%` }}\n   382→                />\n   383→                <div className=\"flex gap-2 mt-2\">\n   384→                  <div\n   385→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   386→                    style={{\n   387→                      width: \"5rem\",\n   388→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   389→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   390→                    }}\n   391→                  />\n   392→                  <div\n   393→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   394→                    style={{\n   395→                      width: \"4rem\",\n   396→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   397→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   398→                    }}\n   399→                  />\n   400→                  <div\n   401→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   402→                    style={{\n   403→                      width: \"5.5rem\",\n   404→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   405→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   406→                    }}\n   407→                  />\n   408→                </div>\n   409→              </div>\n   410→            </div>\n   411→          ))}\n   412→        </div>\n   413→      ) : error ? (\n   414→        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   415→          <svg\n   416→            width=\"40\"\n   417→            height=\"40\"\n   418→            viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   419→            fill=\"none\"\n   420→            stroke=\"var(--color-error)\"\n   421→            strokeWidth={1.5}\n   422→            style={{ margin: \"0 auto 0.75rem\" }}\n   423→          >\n   424→            <circle cx={12} cy={12} r={10} />\n   425→            <line x1={12} y1={8} x2={12} y2={12} />\n   426→            <line x1={12} y1={16} x2={12.01} y2={16} />\n   427→          </svg>\n   428→          <p\n   429→            className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n   430→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   431→          >\n   432→            {error}\n   433→          </p>\n   434→          <button\n   435→            onClick={() => fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom)}\n   436→            className=\"btn-primary\"\n   437→            style={{ marginTop: \"0.75rem\" }}\n   438→          >\n   439→            Try again\n   440→          </button>\n   441→        </div>\n   442→      ) : listings.length === 0 ? 64→  const [showManualFallback, setShowManualFallback] = useState(false);\n    65→  // Discord state\n    66→  const [discordLink, setDiscordLink] = useState(\"\");\n    67→  const [discordSaving, setDiscordSaving] = useState(false);\n    68→  const [autoDiscordLoading, setAutoDiscordLoading] = useState(false);\n    69→  const [autoDiscordLink, setAutoDiscordLink] = useState(\"\");\n    70→ setGivenRatings] = useState<Record<number, { score: number; comment: string }>>({});\n    75→  const [ratingScores, setRatingScores] = useState<Record<number, number>>({});\n    76→  const [ratingComments, setRatingComments] = useState<Record<number, string>>({});\n    77→  const [ratingSaving, setRatingSaving] = useState<number | null>(null);\n    78→  const [ratingMessage, [showDeleteConfirm, setShowDeleteConfirm] = useState(false);\n    84→  const [deleting, setDeleting] = useState(false);\n    85→\n    86→  const fetchListing = useCallback(async () => {\n    87→    try {\n    88→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`);\n    89→      const data = await res.json();\n    90→      if (res.ok && data.listing) {\n    91→        setListing(data.listing);\n    92→        setTelegramLink(data.listing.telegram_link || \"\");\n    93→        setDiscordLink(data.listing.discord_link || \"\");\n    94→      } else if (res.status === 404) {\n    95→        setNotAvailable(true);\n    96→      } else {\n    97→        setFetchError(true);\n    98→      }\n    99→    } catch {\n   100→      setFetchError(true);\n   101→    } finally {\n   102→      setLoading(false);\n   103→    }\n   104→  }, [id]);\n   105→\n   106→  useEffect(() => {\n   107→    fetchListing();\n   108→  }, [fetchListing]);\n   109→\n   110→  const handleDelete = async () => {\n   111→    setDeleting(true);\n   112→    setError(\"\");\n   113→    try {\n   114→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`, { method: \"DELETE\" });\n   115→      const data = await res.json();\n   116→      if (res.ok) {\n   117→        router.push(\"/\");\n   118→      } else {\n   119→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to delete listing\");\n   120→        setShowDeleteConfirm(false);\n   121→      }\n   122→    } catch {\n   123→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n   124→      setShowDeleteConfirm(false);\n   125→    } finally {\n   126→      setDeleting(false);\n   127→    }\n   128→  };\n   129→\n   130→  const fetchRatings = useCallback(async () => {\n   131→    if (!id) return;\n   132→    try {\n   133→      const res = await fetch(`/api/ratings?listingId=${id}`);\n   134→      const data = await res.json();\n   135→      if (data.givenRatings) {\n   136→        const given: Record<number, { score: number; comment: string }> = {};\n   137→        const scores: Record<number, number> = {};\n   138→        const comments: Record<number, string> = {};\n   139→        for (const r of data.givenRatings) {\n   140→          given[r.rated_user_id] = { score: r.score, comment: r.comment };\n   141→          scores[r.rated_user_id] = r.score;\n   142→          comments[r.rated_user_id] = r.comment;\n   143→        }\n   144→        setGivenRatings(given);\n   145→        setRatingScores(scores);\n   146→ {\n   154→    if (listing?.is_full && listing?.isMember) {\n   155→      fetchRatings();\n   156→    }\n   157→  }, [listing?.is_full, listing?.isMember, fetchRatings]);\n   158→\n   159→  const handleRatingSubmit = async (memberId: number) => {\n   160→    const score = ratingScores[memberId];\n   161→    if (!score) return;\n   162→    setRatingSaving(memberId);\n   163→    setRatingMessage(\"\");\n   164→    try {\n   165→      const res = await fetch(\"/api/ratings\", {\n   166→        method: \"POST\",\n   167→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" finally {\n   188→      setRatingSaving(null);\n   189→    }\n   190→  };\n   191→\n   192→  const handleJoin = async () => {\n   193→    setJoining(true);\n   194→    setError(\"\");\n   195→    try {\n   196→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/join`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   197→      const data = await res.json();\n   198→      if (!res.ok) {\n   199→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   200→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01D692zmnNcsB7Rqcev8ZVMP","session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"735c45fb-ebad-43e8-af8f-1f416311a6d7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:01:26.505Z"}

Please try again.\");\n    36→    } finally {\n    37→      setLoading(false);\n    38→    }\n    39→  };\n    40→\n    41→  return (\n    42→    <div className=\"max-w-md mx-auto mt-4 sm:mt-12\">\n    43→      <div className=\"card\">\n    44→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl mb-1\">Welcome back</h1>\n    45→        <p\n    46→          className=\"mb-6\"\n    47→          style={{\n    48→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    49→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    50→            fontSize: \"0.9rem\",\n    51→          }}\n    52→        >\n    53→          Sign in to continue reading with your groups\n    54→        </p>\n    55→\n    56→        {error && (\n    57→          <div\n    58→            className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n    59→            style={{\n    60→              backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n    61→              color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n    62→              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    63→            }}\n    64→            role=\"alert\"\n    65→            id=\"login-error\"\n    66→          >\n    67→            {error}\n    68→          </div>\n    69→        )}\n    70→\n    71→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-4\">\n    72→          <div>\n    73→            <label\n    74→              htmlFor=\"login-email\"\n    75→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    76→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    77→            >\n    78→              Email\n    79→            </label>\n    80→            <input\n    81→              id=\"login-email\"\n    82→              type=\"email\"\n    83→              className=\"input-field\"\n    84→              value={email}\n    85→              onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}\n    86→              required\n    87→              placeholder=\"you@example.com\"\n    88→              autoComplete=\"email\"\n    89→              aria-describedby={error ? \"login-error\" : undefined}\n    90→            />\n    91→          </div>\n    92→\n    93→          <div>\n    94→            <label\n    95→              htmlFor=\"login-password\"\n    96→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    97→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    98→            >\n    99→              Password\n   100→            </label>\n   101→            <input\n   102→              id=\"login-password\"\n   103→              type=\"password\"\n   104→              className=\"input-field\"\n   105→              value={password}\n   106→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}\n   107→              required\n   108→              placeholder=\"Your password\"\n   109→              autoComplete=\"current-password\"\n   110→              aria-describedby={error ? \"login-error\" : undefined}\n   111→            />\n   112→          </div>\n   113→\n   114→          <button\n   115→            type=\"submit\"\n   116→            className=\"btn-primary w-full\"\n   117→            disabled={loading}\n   118→          >\n   119→            {loading ? \"Signing in...\" : \"Sign in\"}\n   120→          </button>\n   121→        </form>\n   122→\n   123→        <p\n   124→          className=\"text-sm text-center mt-4\"\n         color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n    50→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n    51→            fontSize: \"0.9rem\",\n    52→          }}\n    53→        >\n    54→
