sans-serif\",\n    64→            }}\n    65→            role=\"alert\"\n    66→            id=\"register-error\"\n    67→          >\n    68→            {error}\n    69→          </div>\n    70→        )}\n    71→\n    72→        <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-4\">\n    73→          <div>\n    74→            <label\n    75→              htmlFor=\"register-name\"\n    76→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    77→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    78→            >\n    79→              Display name\n    80→            </label>\n    81→            <input\n    82→              id=\"register-name\"\n    83→              type=\"text\"\n    84→              className=\"input-field\"\n    85→              value={displayName}\n    86→              onChange={(e) => setDisplayName(e.target.value)}\n    87→              required\n    88→              placeholder=\"How others will see you\"\n    89→              autoComplete=\"name\"\n    90→              aria-describedby={error ? \"register-error\" : undefined}\n    91→            />\n    92→          </div>\n    93→\n    94→          <div>\n    95→            <label\n    96→              htmlFor=\"register-email\"\n    97→              className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n    98→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n    99→            >\n   100→              Email\n   101→            </label>\n   102→            <input\n   103→              id=\"register-email\"\n   104→              type=\"email\"\n   105→ id=\"register-password\"\n   125→              type=\"password\"\n   126→              className=\"input-field\"\n   127→              value={password}\n   128→              onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}\n   129→              required\n   130→              minLength={8}\n   131→              placeholder=\"At least 8 characters\"\n   132→              autoComplete=\"new-password\"\n   133→              aria-describedby={error ? \"register-error\" : undefined}\n   134→            />\n   135→          </div>\n   136→\n   137→          <button\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01HTovuQZNrgNEZHwZ5Nh5Mx","type":"tool_result","content":"   200→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   201→          return;\n   202→        }\n   203→        setError(data.error);\n   204→        return;\n   205→      }\n   206→      await fetchListing();\n   207→    } catch {\n   208→      setError(\"Failed to join. Please try again.\");\n   209→    } finally {\n   210→      setJoining(false);\n   211→    }\n   212→  };\n   213→\n   214→  const handleApply = async () => {\n   215→    setApplying(true);\n   216→    setError(\"\");\n   217→    try {\n   218→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/apply`, { method: \"POST\" });\n   219→      const data = await res.json();\n   220→      if (!res.ok) {\n   221→        if (res.status === 401) {\n   222→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n   223→          return;\n   224→        }\n   225→        setError(data.error);\n   226→        return;\n   227→      }\n   228→      await fetchListing();\n   229→    } catch {\n   230→      setError(\"Failed to apply. Please try again.\");\n   231→    } finally {\n   232→      setApplying(false);\n   233→    }\n   234→  };\n   235→\n   236→  const handleDecision = async (applicationId: number, action: \"approve\" | \"reject\") => {\n   237→    setDecidingId(applicationId);\n   238→    setError(\"\");\n   239→    try {\n   240→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/applications`, {\n   241→        method: \"PATCH\",\n   242→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   243→        body: JSON.stringify({ applicationId, action }),\n   244→      });\n   245→      const data = await res.json();\n   246→      if (!res.ok) {\n   247→        setError(data.error);\n   248→        return;\n   249→      }\n   250→      await fetchListing();\n   251→    } catch {\n   252→      setError(\"Failed to process application. Please try again.\");\n   253→    } finally {\n   254→      setDecidingId(null);\n   255→    }\n   256→  };\n   257→\n   258→  const isValidTelegramLink = (link: string) => {\n   259→    return /^https:\\/\\/t\\.me\\/[\\w+/]+$/.test(link.trim());\n   260→  };\n   261→\n   262→  const telegramLinkError = telegramLink && !isValidTelegramLink(telegramLink)\n   263→    ? \"Link must start with https://t.me/ (e.g., https://t.me/+AbCdEfGhIjK)\"\n   264→    : \"\";\n   265→\n   266→  const handleTelegramSave = async () => {\n   267→    if (!isValidTelegramLink(telegramLink)) return;\n   268→    setTelegramSaving(true);\n   269→    setError(\"\");\n   270→    try {\n   271→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/telegram`, {\n   272→        method: \"POST\",\n   273→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   274→        body: JSON.stringify({ telegramLink: telegramLink.trim() }),\n   275→      });\n   276→      const data = await res.json();\n   277→      if (!res.ok) {\n   278→        setError(data.error);\n   279→        return;\n   280→      }\n   281→      await fetchListing();\n   282→    } catch {\n   283→      setError(\"Failed to save link. Please try again.\");\n   284→    } finally {\n   285→      setTelegramSaving(false);\n   286→    }\n   287→  };\n   288→\n   289→  const handleAutoTelegram = async () => {\n   290→    setAutoTelegramLoading(true);\n   291→    setError(\"\");\n   292→    try {\n   293→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/auto-telegram`);\n   294→      const data = await res.json();\n   295→      if (!res.ok) {\n   296→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to generate Telegram link\");\n   297→        return;\n   298→      }\n   299→      setAutoTelegramLink(data.deepLink);\n   300→    } catch {\n   301→      setError(\"Failed to connect. Please try again.\");\n   302→    } finally {\n   303→      setAutoTelegramLoading(false);\n   304→    }\n   305→  };\n   306→\n   307→  const isValidDiscordLink = (link: string) => {\n   308→    return /^https:\\/\\/(discord\\.gg|discord\\.com\\/invite)\\/[\\w-]+$/.test(link.trim());\n   309→  };\n   310→\n   311→  const discordLinkError = discordLink && !isValidDiscordLink(discordLink)\n   312→    ? \"Link must start with https://discord.gg/ or https://discord.com/invite/\"\n   313→    : \"\";\n   314→\n   315→  const handleDiscordSave = async () => {\n   316→    if (!isValidDiscordLink(discordLink)) return;\n   317→    setDiscordSaving(true);\n   318→    setError(\"\");\n   319→    try {\n   320→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/discord`, {\n   321→        method: \"POST\",\n   322→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n   323→        body: JSON.stringify({ discordLink: discordLink.trim() }),\n   324→      });\n   325→      const data = await res.json();\n   326→      if (!res.ok) {\n   327→        setError(data.error);\n   328→        return;\n   329→      }\n   330→      await fetchListing();\n   331→    } catch {\n   332→      setError(\"Failed to save link. Please try again.\");\n   333→    } finally {\n   334→      setDiscordSaving(false);\n   335→    }\n   336→  };\n   337→\n   338→  const handleAutoDiscord = async () => {\n   339→    setAutoDiscordLoading(true);\n   340→    setError(\"\");\n   341→    try {\n   342→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}/auto-discord`);\n   343→      const data = await res.json();\n   344→      if (!res.ok) {\n   345→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to generate Discord link\");\n   346→        return;\n   347→      }\n   348→      setAutoDiscordLink(data.inviteUrl);\n   349→    } catch {\n   350→      setError(\"Failed to connect. Please try again.\");\n   351→    } finally {\n   352→      setAutoDiscordLoading(false);\n   353→    }\n   354→  };\n   355→\n   356→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   357→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   358→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   359→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   360→      mixed: \"Mixed (voice + text)\",\n   361→    };\n   362→    return labels[f] || f;\n   363→  };\n   364→\n   365→  if (loading) {\n   366→    return (\n   367→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n   368→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n   369→      </div>\n   370→    );\n   371→  }\n   372→\n   373→  if (fetchError) {\n   374→    return (\n   375→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   376→        <h2 className=\"text-2xl mb-2\">Something went wrong</h2>\n   377→        <p style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>\n   378→          Failed to load this listing. which tabs have been fetched to avoid redundant requests\n    70→  const fetchedTabs = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set());\n    71→\n    72→  useEffect(() => {\n    73→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    74→ <h3 className=\"text-lg mb-3\">Recent Feedback</h3>\n   460→                  <div className=\"space-y-3\">\n   461→                    {reputation.recentRatings.map((rating, idx) => (\n   462→ (\n   546→            <div className=\"text-center py-4\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n   547→              <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n   548→            </div>\n   549→          ) : (\n   550→            <>\n   551→              <div className=\"flex flex-wrap gap-2\">\n   552→                {availableGenres.map((genre) => {\n   553→                  const selected = userGenres.includes(genre);\n   554→                  return (\n   555→                    <button\n   556→                      key={genre}\n   557→                      onClick={() => toggleGenre(genre)}\n   558→                      aria-pressed={selected}\n   559→                      className=\"px-3 py-1.5 rounded-full text-sm font-medium transition-all\"\n   560→                      style={{\n   561→                        fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   562→                        backgroundColor: selected ? `\\u2713 ${genre}` : genre}\n   569→                    </button>\n   570→                  );\n   571→                })}\n   572→              </div>\n   573→\n   574→              {userGenres.length > 0 && (\n   575→                <p\n   576→                  className=\"text-sm\"\n   577→                  style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   578→                >\n   579→                  {userGenres.length} genre{userGenres.length !== 1 ? \"s\" : \"\"} selected\n   580→                </p>\n   581→              )}\n   582→\n   583→              {genresMessage && (\n   584→                <p\n   585→                  className=\"text-sm\"\n   586→                  style={{\n   587→                    color: genresMessage.includes(\"saved\")\n   588→                      ? (\n   650→        <div className=\"card\">\n   651→          <p\n   652→            className=\"text-sm text-center py-4\"\n   653→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   654→          >\n   655→            {emptyText}\n   656→          </p>\n   657→        </div>\n   658→      ) : (\n   659→        <div className=\"space-y-3\">\n   660→          {items.map((item) => (\n   661→            <Link\n   662→              key={item.id}\n   663→              href={`/listings/${item.id}`}\n   664→              className=\"card flex gap-4 items-start hover:shadow-md transition-shadow\"\n   665→              style={{ display: \"flex\", textDecoration: \"none\", color: \"inherit\" }}\n   666→            >\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01EUbKNdrvHW91JVwSsMxhVh","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useState, useEffect } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useParams, useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Image from \"next/image\";\n     6→\n     7→export default function EditListingPage() {\n     8→  const { id } = useParams();\n     9→  const router = useRouter();\n    10→  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n    11→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false);\n    12→  const [fetching, setFetching] = useState(true);\n    13→\n    14→  const [bookTitle, setBookTitle] = useState(\"\");\n    15→  const [bookAuthor, setBookAuthor] = useState(\"\");\n    16→  const [bookCoverUrl, setBookCoverUrl] = useState(\"\");\n    17→  const [language, setLanguage] = useState(\"English\");\n    18→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    19→  const [startDate, setStartDate] = useState(\"\");\n    20→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"text\");\n    21→  const [maxGroupSize, setMaxGroupSize] = useState(\"4\");\n    22→  const [requiresApproval, setRequiresApproval] = useState(false);\n    23→  const [platformPreference, setPlatformPreference] = useState(\"telegram\");\n    24→  const [memberCount, setMemberCount] = useState(1);\n    25→\n    26→  useEffect(() => {\n    27→    async function fetchListing() {\n    28→      try {\n    29→        const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`);\n    30→        const data = await res.json();\n    31→        if (res.ok && data.listing) {\n    32→          const l = data.listing;\n    33→          if (!l.isAuthor) {\n    34→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    35→            return;\n    36→          }\n    37→          // Cannot edit if platform link shared\n    38→          if (l.telegram_link || l.discord_link) {\n    39→            router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    40→            return;\n    41→          }\n    42→          setBookTitle(l.book_title);\n    43→          setBookAuthor(l.book_author);\n    44→          setBookCoverUrl(l.book_cover_url || \"\");\n    45→          setLanguage(l.language || \"English\");\n    46→          setReadingPace(l.reading_pace || \"\");\n    47→          setStartDate(l.start_date || \"\");\n    48→          setMeetingFormat(l.meeting_format || \"text\");\n    49→          setMaxGroupSize(String(l.max_group_size || 4));\n    50→          setRequiresApproval(!!l.requires_approval);\n    51→          setPlatformPreference(l.platform_preference || \"telegram\");\n    52→          setMemberCount(l.memberCount || 1);\n    53→        } else if (res.status === 401) {\n    54→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    55→        } else {\n    56→          setError(\"Listing not found\");\n    57→        }\n    58→      } catch {\n    59→        setError(\"Failed to load listing\");\n    60→      } finally {\n    61→        setFetching(false);\n    62→      }\n    63→    }\n    64→    fetchListing();\n    65→  }, [id, router]);\n    66→\n    67→  const handleSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {\n    68→    e.preventDefault();\n    69→    setError(\"\");\n    70→    setLoading(true);\n    71→\n    72→    try {\n    73→      const res = await fetch(`/api/listings/${id}`, {\n    74→        method: \"PATCH\",\n    75→        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    76→        body: JSON.stringify({\n    77→          language,\n    78→          readingPace,\n    79→          startDate,\n    80→          meetingFormat,\n    81→          maxGroupSize,\n    82→          requiresApproval,\n    83→          platformPreference,\n    84→        }),\n    85→      });\n    86→\n    87→      const data = await res.json();\n    88→\n    89→      if (!res.ok) {\n    90→        if (res.status === 401) {\n    91→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    92→          return;\n    93→        }\n    94→        setError(data.error || \"Failed to update listing\");\n    95→        return;\n    96→      }\n    97→\n    98→      router.push(`/listings/${id}`);\n    99→    } catch {\n   100→      setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n   101→    } finally {\n   102→      setLoading(false);\n   103→    }\n   104→  };\n   105→\n   106→  if (fetching) {\n   107→    return (\n   108→      <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n   109→        <div className=\"animate-pulse space-y-4\">\n   110→          <div className=\"h-8 bg-gray-200 rounded w-2/3\" />\n   111→          <div className=\"h-4 bg-gray-200 rounded w-1/2\" />\n   112→          <div className=\"h-40 bg-gray-200 rounded\" />\n   113→        </div>\n   114→      </div>\n   115→    );\n   116→  }\n   117→\n   118→  // Filter group size options: only allow sizes >= current member count\n   119→  const groupSizeOptions = [2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 15, 20].filter(\n   120→    (n) => n >= memberCount\n   121→  );\n   122→\n   123→  return (\n   124→    <div className=\"max-w-lg mx-auto\">\n   125→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-2\">Edit reading group</h1>\n   126→      <p\n   127→        className=\"mb-6 sm:mb-8\"\n   128→        style={{\n   129→          color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   130→          fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   131→          fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n   132→        }}\n   133→      >\n   134→        Update your group settings. Book title and author cannot be changed.\n   135→      </p>\n   136→\n   137→      {error && (\n   138→        <div\n   139→          className=\"mb-4 p-3 rounded-lg text-sm\"\n   140→          style={{\n   141→            backgroundColor: \"rgba(192, 57, 43, 0.08)\",\n   142→            color: \"var(--color-error)\",\n   143→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   144→          }}\n   145→          role=\"alert\"\n   146→        >\n   147→          {error}\n   148→        </div>\n   149→      )}\n   150→\n   151→      {/* Book info (read-only) */}\n   152→      <div\n   153→        className=\"mb-6 flex items-center gap-3 p-4 rounded-lg\"\n   154→        style={{\n   155→          backgroundColor: \"rgba(224, 122, 58, 0.06)\",\n   156→          border: \"1px solid var(--color-border)\",\n   157→        }}\n   158→      >\n   159→        {bookCoverUrl && (\n   160→          <Image\n   161→            src={bookCoverUrl}\n   162→            alt={`Cover of ${bookTitle}`}\n   163→            width={48}\n   164→            height={68}\n   165→            className=\"w-12 h-17 object-cover rounded\"\n   166→          />\n   167→        )}\n   168→        <div>\n   169→          <div\n   170→            className=\"text-sm font-semibold\"\n   171→            style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   172→          >\n   173→    <form onSubmit={handleSubmit} className=\"space-y-5\">\n   198→        <div>\n   199→          <label\n   200→            className=\"block text-sm font-semibold mb-1\"\n   201→            style={{ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TgHwyr3iD3LR3DmvwBh2f6","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→\n     6→interface Notification {\n     7→  id: number;\n     8→  listing_id: number;\n     9→  type: string;\n    10→  message: string;\n    11→  is_read: number;\n    12→  created_at: string;\n    13→  book_title: string;\n    14→}\n    15→\n    16→export default function NotificationsPage() {\n    17→  const [notifications, setNotifications] = useState<Notification[]>([]);\n    18→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    19→\n    20→  useEffect(() => {\n    21→    fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    22→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    23→      .then((data) => {\n    24→        setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n    25→        setLoading(false);\n    26→        // Mark all as read\n    27→        fetch(\"/api/notifications\", {\n    28→          method: \"PATCH\",\n    29→          headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\n    30→          body: JSON.stringify({}),\n    31→        });\n    32→      });\n    33→  }, []);\n    34→\n    35→  if (loading) {\n    36→    return (\n    37→      <div className=\"text-center py-16\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}>\n    38→        <p style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}>Loading...</p>\n    39→      </div>\n    40→    );\n    41→  }\n    42→\n    43→  return (\n    44→    <div className=\"max-w-2xl mx-auto\">\n    45→      <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-3xl mb-4 sm:mb-6\">Notifications</h1>\n    46→\n    47→      {notifications.length === 0 ? {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D692zmnNcsB7Rqcev8ZVMP","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a complete picture. Here is the review.\n\n---\n\n## Client-Side Code Review — Bookmate\n\nFiles reviewed:\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/Navbar.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookCover.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Race condition in `fetchListings` — stale responses can overwrite newer ones\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 44–76\n\n`fetchListings` is a plain promise chain with no cancellation. When a user types a search query followed quickly by a filter change, two fetches are in flight simultaneously. If the earlier (slower) request resolves last, it overwrites the state from the newer request, displaying wrong results with no indication anything went wrong.\n\nThe debounce on the search input (line 88) mitigates this for search-only changes, but every filter control and page change calls `fetchListings` immediately and directly, with no debounce or abort. Rapid clicking on page \"Next\" multiple times, or changing the format filter right after the sort filter, will reliably trigger this.\n\nFix: track a request ID or use `AbortController` inside `fetchListings`, ignoring responses from superseded requests. The `BookSearch` component (`BookSearch.tsx` lines 55–83) already demonstrates the correct pattern.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `start_date` parsed as UTC, displayed in local time — date is always off by one in negative-offset timezones\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, line 565\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 461 and 504\n\nDates stored as `YYYY-MM-DD` strings (e.g. `\"2026-04-01\"`) are passed directly to `new Date()`. The `Date` constructor interprets bare ISO date strings as UTC midnight, so `new Date(\"2026-04-01\")` is `2026-03-31T23:00:00` in UTC-1 and `2026-03-31T20:00:00` in UTC-4. When then formatted with `toLocaleDateString`, users in any timezone west of UTC will see a date one day earlier than intended.\n\nThe comparison on ListingDetail.tsx line 461 (`new Date(listing.start_date) < new Date(new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0])`) is partially protected because both sides are in UTC, but the display paths on lines 504 and page.tsx 565 are not.\n\nFix: Parse the date string with explicit UTC interpretation:\n```ts\n// Replace: new Date(listing.start_date).toLocaleDateString(...)\n// With:\nnew Date(listing.start_date + \"T00:00:00Z\").toLocaleDateString(...)\n// Or:\nconst [y, m, d] = listing.start_date.split(\"-\").map(Number);\nnew Date(y, m - 1, d).toLocaleDateString(...)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. `next/image` used without `unoptimized` for user-supplied cover URLs in `edit/page.tsx`\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`, lines 160–167\n\nThe edit page renders the book cover using `<Image>` from `next/image` (not the `BookCover` component) without the `unoptimized` prop. The `bookCoverUrl` value comes from `data.listing.book_cover_url`, which is ultimately whatever URL was submitted when the listing was created — an Open Library cover URL. This goes through Next.js image optimization.\n\nThe `next.config.ts` only allows `covers.openlibrary.org` and `archive.org` as remote patterns. If a cover URL from any other domain was somehow stored (e.g., from an older record, a manual DB edit, or a future API change), this will throw a runtime error (\"hostname not configured\"). By contrast, `BookCover.tsx` uses `unoptimized` to bypass this entirely, which is the established pattern everywhere else. The create page (line 123) also uses raw `<Image>` without `unoptimized`.\n\nFix: Use the `BookCover` component, or add `unoptimized` to both raw `<Image>` instances in `create/page.tsx` (line 123) and `edit/page.tsx` (line 160), consistent with how `BookCover.tsx` handles it.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Notifications page: fetch errors are silently swallowed and loading spinner hangs\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/page.tsx`, lines 20–33\n\nThe `useEffect` has no `.catch()` on the outer fetch. If the `/api/notifications` request fails (network error, 401, 500), the promise rejection is unhandled and `setLoading(false)` is never called. The user sees the loading state indefinitely with no error message and no way to retry.\n\nAdditionally, the mark-as-read `PATCH` request on lines 27–31 fires inside the `.then()` with no error handling at all — a failure there is completely silent, meaning the notification badge count in the Navbar will never decrement even after the user views their notifications.\n\nFix:\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    .then((r) => {\n      if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load\");\n      return r.json();\n    })\n    .then((data) => {\n      setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n      // fire-and-forget PATCH is acceptable, but add .catch(() => {})\n      fetch(\"/api/notifications\", { method: \"PATCH\", ... }).catch(() => {});\n    })\n    .catch(() => {\n      setError(\"Could not load notifications.\");\n    })\n    .finally(() => setLoading(false));\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. `button` with `role=\"option\"` inside a `listbox` — invalid ARIA structure\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 180–227\n\nThe listbox dropdown renders each result as a `<button role=\"option\">`. The ARIA spec requires that `role=\"option\"` be a direct child of a `role=\"listbox\"` element, and that `option` elements not be interactive elements (`button`, `a`, etc.) — they should be non-interactive elements like `div` or `li`. Screen readers that follow the spec strictly (notably JAWS and some NVDA configurations) will not correctly announce these as options in a combobox pattern; keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements will be broken for assistive technology users.\n\nFix: Change the `<button>` to a `<div>` with `role=\"option\"`, `tabIndex={-1}`, and handle click/keyboard interaction through the parent combobox input's `onKeyDown` (which is already implemented). The `listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex]` scroll logic will continue to work.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Edit listing page: `maxGroupSize` state initialized to `\"4\"` but may never reflect the actual current value before the form is submitted in a fast navigation\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`, lines 20, 48–49, 67–104\n\nWhen the user navigates to the edit page, the form fields are all initialized with placeholder defaults (`maxGroupSize = \"4\"`, `meetingFormat = \"text\"`, etc.) on lines 19–23 and then overwritten by `setMaxGroupSize(String(l.max_group_size || 4))` inside the async `fetchListing`. However, `handleSubmit` at line 67 can be called before `fetchListing` resolves if the user somehow triggers a submit while the `fetching` state is still `true`.\n\nWhile the page renders a loading skeleton while `fetching` is true (line 106) and the submit button is only shown in the form below (line 466), the `fetching` guard only controls what is rendered — the form does not actually exist in the DOM while `fetching` is `true`, so a submit cannot be triggered during that window. However, this is fragile: if `setFetching(false)` is called before the state setters finish in a concurrent render scenario, the form can appear with default values for a brief frame.\n\nMore concretely: if the fetch resolves but the listing is not found (`setError(\"Listing not found\")`), `setFetching(false)` is still called, the form is now shown, and it is populated with default values (`\"4\"` readers, `\"text\"` format). Submitting this would silently patch the listing with wrong data. The error message is shown, but the form is fully interactive beneath it. The submit button should also be disabled or the form hidden when there's a fetch error. **Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 7. `profile/page.tsx`: auth check does not redirect on network failure — user can view empty profile form\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, lines 72–88\n\nThe auth check on line 72 calls `/api/auth/me` and redirects to `/auth/login` only if `data.user` is falsy. If the fetch itself rejects (network error, timeout), the `.catch()` at line 85 calls `setLoading(false)` without redirecting or setting an error state. The user then sees an empty form with blank email and displayName fields, and can attempt to submit — the `handleSave` will then POST empty `displayName` and `bio` to `/api/profile`, which will either fail on the server or succeed in clearing their profile data.\n\nFix: In the `.catch()`, redirect to `/auth/login` or show a clear error state rather than just `setLoading(false)`.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\n| # | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|-------|------|-----------|\n| 1 | Race condition: concurrent `fetchListings` calls, stale results overwrite newer ones | `app/page.tsx` | 95 |\n| 2 | `start_date` date-off-by-one in negative UTC offset timezones | `page.tsx:565`, `ListingDetail.tsx:504,461` | 92 |\n| 3 | Raw `<Image>` without `unoptimized` in create/edit pages, inconsistent with `BookCover` pattern | `create/page.tsx:123`, `edit/page.tsx:160` | 88 |\n| 4 | Notifications page: no error handling on outer fetch — infinite loading spinner on failure | `notifications/page.tsx:20` | 88 |\n| 5 | `<button role=\"option\">` is invalid ARIA — screen reader combobox interaction broken | `BookSearch.tsx:180` | 85 |\n| 6 | Edit page: form shown with default values when fetch returns an error; submittable with wrong data | `edit/page.tsx:53–58` | 82 |\n| 7 | Profile auth guard: network error leaves user on blank form instead of redirecting | `profile/page.tsx:85` | 82 |"},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: ae66798dcadf1171a (use SendMessage with to: 'ae66798dcadf1171a' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 83207\ntool_uses: 28\nduration_ms: 117038</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"edb2a5b9-962a-43da-80eb-9f8f7e8c3722","uuid":"14421226-c7e1-448e-94c8-f9d88ac7309b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T06:03:11.674Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the client-side React components and pages in the Bookmate Next.js app for bugs, logic errors, UX issues, and code quality problems. Focus on:\n\n1. React components in `app/components/`\n2. Page components in `app/app/` (especially client-side logic, state management, form handling)\n3. Look for: memory leaks, race conditions, missing error handling, incorrect state updates, accessibility gaps, XSS vectors in rendered content, missing loading/error states\n\nThe project is at /root/Projects/bookmate/app. This is a Next.js 15 App Router project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS v4, iron-session auth.\n\nPrevious code reviews (8 rounds) focused heavily on API routes. This review should focus on the CLIENT-SIDE code: components, pages, hooks, event handlers, form submissions, client-side data fetching.\n\nReport only HIGH CONFIDENCE bugs and issues. Skip cosmetic/style opinions.","agentId":"ae66798dcadf1171a","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a complete picture. Here is the review.\n\n---\n\n## Client-Side Code Review — Bookmate\n\nFiles reviewed:\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/Navbar.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookCover.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/create/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/page.tsx`\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`\n\n---\n\n## Critical Issues\n\n### 1. Race condition in `fetchListings` — stale responses can overwrite newer ones\n\n**Confidence: 95**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, lines 44–76\n\n`fetchListings` is a plain promise chain with no cancellation. When a user types a search query followed quickly by a filter change, two fetches are in flight simultaneously. If the earlier (slower) request resolves last, it overwrites the state from the newer request, displaying wrong results with no indication anything went wrong.\n\nThe debounce on the search input (line 88) mitigates this for search-only changes, but every filter control and page change calls `fetchListings` immediately and directly, with no debounce or abort. Rapid clicking on page \"Next\" multiple times, or changing the format filter right after the sort filter, will reliably trigger this.\n\nFix: track a request ID or use `AbortController` inside `fetchListings`, ignoring responses from superseded requests. The `BookSearch` component (`BookSearch.tsx` lines 55–83) already demonstrates the correct pattern.\n\n---\n\n### 2. `start_date` parsed as UTC, displayed in local time — date is always off by one in negative-offset timezones\n\n**Confidence: 92**\n\n**Files:**\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/page.tsx`, line 565\n- `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/ListingDetail.tsx`, lines 461 and 504\n\nDates stored as `YYYY-MM-DD` strings (e.g. `\"2026-04-01\"`) are passed directly to `new Date()`. The `Date` constructor interprets bare ISO date strings as UTC midnight, so `new Date(\"2026-04-01\")` is `2026-03-31T23:00:00` in UTC-1 and `2026-03-31T20:00:00` in UTC-4. When then formatted with `toLocaleDateString`, users in any timezone west of UTC will see a date one day earlier than intended.\n\nThe comparison on ListingDetail.tsx line 461 (`new Date(listing.start_date) < new Date(new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0])`) is partially protected because both sides are in UTC, but the display paths on lines 504 and page.tsx 565 are not.\n\nFix: Parse the date string with explicit UTC interpretation:\n```ts\n// Replace: new Date(listing.start_date).toLocaleDateString(...)\n// With:\nnew Date(listing.start_date + \"T00:00:00Z\").toLocaleDateString(...)\n// Or:\nconst [y, m, d] = listing.start_date.split(\"-\").map(Number);\nnew Date(y, m - 1, d).toLocaleDateString(...)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 3. `next/image` used without `unoptimized` for user-supplied cover URLs in `edit/page.tsx`\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`, lines 160–167\n\nThe edit page renders the book cover using `<Image>` from `next/image` (not the `BookCover` component) without the `unoptimized` prop. The `bookCoverUrl` value comes from `data.listing.book_cover_url`, which is ultimately whatever URL was submitted when the listing was created — an Open Library cover URL. This goes through Next.js image optimization.\n\nThe `next.config.ts` only allows `covers.openlibrary.org` and `archive.org` as remote patterns. If a cover URL from any other domain was somehow stored (e.g., from an older record, a manual DB edit, or a future API change), this will throw a runtime error (\"hostname not configured\"). By contrast, `BookCover.tsx` uses `unoptimized` to bypass this entirely, which is the established pattern everywhere else. The create page (line 123) also uses raw `<Image>` without `unoptimized`.\n\nFix: Use the `BookCover` component, or add `unoptimized` to both raw `<Image>` instances in `create/page.tsx` (line 123) and `edit/page.tsx` (line 160), consistent with how `BookCover.tsx` handles it.\n\n---\n\n### 4. Notifications page: fetch errors are silently swallowed and loading spinner hangs\n\n**Confidence: 88**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/notifications/page.tsx`, lines 20–33\n\nThe `useEffect` has no `.catch()` on the outer fetch. If the `/api/notifications` request fails (network error, 401, 500), the promise rejection is unhandled and `setLoading(false)` is never called. The user sees the loading state indefinitely with no error message and no way to retry.\n\nAdditionally, the mark-as-read `PATCH` request on lines 27–31 fires inside the `.then()` with no error handling at all — a failure there is completely silent, meaning the notification badge count in the Navbar will never decrement even after the user views their notifications.\n\nFix:\n```ts\nuseEffect(() => {\n  fetch(\"/api/notifications\")\n    .then((r) => {\n      if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load\");\n      return r.json();\n    })\n    .then((data) => {\n      setNotifications(data.notifications || []);\n      // fire-and-forget PATCH is acceptable, but add .catch(() => {})\n      fetch(\"/api/notifications\", { method: \"PATCH\", ... }).catch(() => {});\n    })\n    .catch(() => {\n      setError(\"Could not load notifications.\");\n    })\n    .finally(() => setLoading(false));\n}, []);\n```\n\n---\n\n## Important Issues\n\n### 5. `button` with `role=\"option\"` inside a `listbox` — invalid ARIA structure\n\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/components/BookSearch.tsx`, lines 180–227\n\nThe listbox dropdown renders each result as a `<button role=\"option\">`. The ARIA spec requires that `role=\"option\"` be a direct child of a `role=\"listbox\"` element, and that `option` elements not be interactive elements (`button`, `a`, etc.) — they should be non-interactive elements like `div` or `li`. Screen readers that follow the spec strictly (notably JAWS and some NVDA configurations) will not correctly announce these as options in a combobox pattern; keyboard navigation and screen reader announcements will be broken for assistive technology users.\n\nFix: Change the `<button>` to a `<div>` with `role=\"option\"`, `tabIndex={-1}`, and handle click/keyboard interaction through the parent combobox input's `onKeyDown` (which is already implemented). The `listboxRef.current.children[activeIndex]` scroll logic will continue to work.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Edit listing page: `maxGroupSize` state initialized to `\"4\"` but may never reflect the actual current value before the form is submitted in a fast navigation\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/listings/[id]/edit/page.tsx`, lines 20, 48–49, 67–104\n\nWhen the user navigates to the edit page, the form fields are all initialized with placeholder defaults (`maxGroupSize = \"4\"`, `meetingFormat = \"text\"`, etc.) on lines 19–23 and then overwritten by `setMaxGroupSize(String(l.max_group_size || 4))` inside the async `fetchListing`. However, `handleSubmit` at line 67 can be called before `fetchListing` resolves if the user somehow triggers a submit while the `fetching` state is still `true`.\n\nWhile the page renders a loading skeleton while `fetching` is true (line 106) and the submit button is only shown in the form below (line 466), the `fetching` guard only controls what is rendered — the form does not actually exist in the DOM while `fetching` is `true`, so a submit cannot be triggered during that window. However, this is fragile: if `setFetching(false)` is called before the state setters finish in a concurrent render scenario, the form can appear with default values for a brief frame.\n\nMore concretely: if the fetch resolves but the listing is not found (`setError(\"Listing not found\")`), `setFetching(false)` is still called, the form is now shown, and it is populated with default values (`\"4\"` readers, `\"text\"` format). Submitting this would silently patch the listing with wrong data. The error message is shown, but the form is fully interactive beneath it. The submit button should also be disabled or the form hidden when there's a fetch error. **Confidence: 82**\n\n---\n\n### 7. `profile/page.tsx`: auth check does not redirect on network failure — user can view empty profile form\n\n**Confidence: 82**\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/profile/page.tsx`, lines 72–88\n\nThe auth check on line 72 calls `/api/auth/me` and redirects to `/auth/login` only if `data.user` is falsy. If the fetch itself rejects (network error, timeout), the `.catch()` at line 85 calls `setLoading(false)` without redirecting or setting an error state. The user then sees an empty form with blank email and displayName fields, and can attempt to submit — the `handleSave` will then POST empty `displayName` and `bio` to `/api/profile`, which will either fail on the server or succeed in clearing their profile data.\n\nFix: In the `.catch()`, redirect to `/auth/login` or show a clear error state rather than just `setLoading(false)`.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\n| # | Issue | File | Confidence |\n|---|-------|------|-----------|\n| 1 | Race condition: concurrent `fetchListings` calls, stale results overwrite newer ones | `app/page.tsx` | 95 |\n| 2 | `start_date` date-off-by-one in negative UTC offset timezones | `page.tsx:565`, `ListingDetail.tsx:504,461` | 92 |\n| 3 | Raw `<Image>` without `unoptimized` in create/edit pages, inconsistent with `BookCover` pattern | `create/page.tsx:123`, `edit/page.tsx:160` | 88 |\n| 4 | Notifications page: no error handling on outer fetch — infinite loading spinner on failure | `notifications/page.tsx:20` | 88 |\n| 5 | `<button role=\"option\">` is invalid ARIA — screen reader combobox interaction broken | `BookSearch.tsx:180` | 85 |\n| 6 | Edit page: form shown with default values when fetch returns an error; submittable with wrong data | `edit/page.tsx:53–58` | 82 |\n| 7 | Profile auth guard: network error leaves user on blank form instead of redirecting | `profile/page.tsx:85` | 82 |"}],"totalDurationMs":117038,"totalTokens":83207,"totalToolUseCount":28,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":433,"cache_read_input_tokens":79883,"output_tokens":2890,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":433},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Mdn5bs5D4ZU7bg44NNMvyo","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     5→import BookCover from \"@/components/BookCover\";\n     6→\n     7→interface Listing {\n     8→  id: number;\n     9→  book_title: string;\n    10→  book_author: string;\n    11→  book_cover_url: string;\n    12→  language: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  max_group_size: number;\n    17→  requires_approval: number;\n    18→  platform_preference: string;\n    19→  member_count: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  created_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→export default function HomePage() {\n    25→  const [listings, setListings] = useState<Listing[]>([]);\n    26→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    27→  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);\n    28→\n    29→  // Search & filter state\n    30→  const [searchQuery, setSearchQuery] = useState(\"\");\n    31→  const [meetingFormat, setMeetingFormat] = useState(\"\");\n    32→  const [readingPace, setReadingPace] = useState(\"\");\n    33→  const [startDateFrom, setStartDateFrom] = useState(\"\");\n    34→  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"newest\");\n    35→  const [filtersOpen, setFiltersOpen] = useState(false);\n    36→\n    37→  // Pagination state\n    38→  const [page, setPage] = useState(1);\n    39→  const [totalPages, setTotalPages] = useState(1);\n    40→  const [totalResults, setTotalResults] = useState(0);\n    41→\n    42→  const debounceRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined);\n    43→\n    44→  const fetchListings = useCallback(\n    45→    (query: string, format: string, sortBy: string, pace?: string, dateFrom?: string, pageNum?: number) => {\n    46→      setLoading(true);\n    47→      const params = new URLSearchParams();\n    48→      if (query) params.set(\"q\", query);\n    49→      if (format) params.set(\"meeting_format\", format);\n    50→      if (pace) params.set(\"reading_pace\", pace);\n    51→      if (dateFrom) params.set(\"start_date_from\", dateFrom);\n    52→      if (sortBy) params.set(\"sort\", sortBy);\n    53→      if (pageNum && pageNum > 1) params.set(\"page\", String(pageNum));\n    54→\n    55→      const qs = params.toString();\n    56→      fetch(`/api/listings${qs ? `?${qs}` : \"\"}`)\n    57→        .then((r) => {\n    58→          if (!r.ok) throw new Error(\"Failed to load listings\");\n    59→          return r.json();\n    60→        })\n    61→        .then((data) => {\n    62→          setListings(data.listings || []);\n    63→          if (data.pagination) {\n    64→            setTotalPages(data.pagination.totalPages || 1);\n    65→            setTotalResults(data.pagination.total || 0);\n    66→          }\n    67→          setError(null);\n    68→          setLoading(false);\n    69→        })\n    70→        .catch((err) => {\n    71→          setError(err.message || \"Could not load listings. Please try again.\");\n    72→          setLoading(false);\n    73→        });\n    74→    },\n    75→    []\n    76→  );\n    77→\n    78→  // Initial fetch\n    79→  useEffect(() => {\n    80→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n    81→  }, [fetchListings]);\n    82→\n    83→  // Debounced search\n    84→  const handleSearchChange = (value: string) => {\n    85→    setSearchQuery(value);\n    86→    setPage(1);\n    87→    if (debounceRef.current) clearTimeout(debounceRef.current);\n    88→    debounceRef.current = setTimeout(() => {\n    89→      fetchListings(value, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    90→    }, 400);\n    91→  };\n    92→\n    93→  // Immediate filter changes\n    94→  const handleFormatChange = (value: string) => {\n    95→    setMeetingFormat(value);\n    96→    setPage(1);\n    97→    fetchListings(searchQuery, value, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n    98→  };\n    99→\n   100→  const handlePaceChange = (value: string) => {\n   101→    setReadingPace(value);\n   102→    setPage(1);\n   103→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, value, startDateFrom, 1);\n   104→  };\n   105→\n   106→  const handleStartDateFromChange = (value: string) => {\n   107→    setStartDateFrom(value);\n   108→    setPage(1);\n   109→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, value, 1);\n   110→  };\n   111→\n   112→  const handleSortChange = (value: string) => {\n   113→    setSort(value);\n   114→    setPage(1);\n   115→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, value, readingPace, startDateFrom, 1);\n   116→  };\n   117→\n   118→  const handlePageChange = (newPage: number) => {\n   119→    setPage(newPage);\n   120→    fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom, newPage);\n   121→    window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: \"smooth\" });\n   122→  };\n   123→\n   124→  const clearFilters = () => {\n   125→    setSearchQuery(\"\");\n   126→    setMeetingFormat(\"\");\n   127→    setReadingPace(\"\");\n   128→    setStartDateFrom(\"\");\n   129→    setSort(\"newest\");\n   130→    setPage(1);\n   131→    fetchListings(\"\", \"\", \"newest\", \"\", \"\", 1);\n   132→  };\n   133→\n   134→  const hasActiveFilters = searchQuery || meetingFormat || readingPace || startDateFrom || sort !== \"newest\";\n   135→\n   136→  const formatMeetingType = (f: string) => {\n   137→    const labels: Record<string, string> = {\n   138→      voice: \"Voice calls\",\n   139→      text: \"Text chat\",\n   140→      mixed: \"Mixed\",\n   141→    };\n   142→    return labels[f] || f;\n   143→  };\n   144→\n   145→  return (\n   146→    <div>\n   147→      <div className=\"text-center mb-6 sm:mb-8 mt-2 sm:mt-4\">\n   148→        <h1 className=\"text-2xl sm:text-4xl mb-2 sm:mb-3\" style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\" }}>\n   149→          Find your reading companion\n   150→        </h1>\n   151→        <p\n   152→          className=\"text-sm sm:text-lg max-w-xl mx-auto\"\n   153→          style={{\n   154→            color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   155→            fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   156→          }}\n   157→        >\n   158→          Browse open reading groups, join one that matches your pace, and\n   159→          connect on Telegram to read together.\n   160→        </p>\n   161→      </div>\n   162→\n   163→      {/* Search & Filter Bar */}\n   164→      <div className=\"mb-6\">\n   165→        <div className=\"flex gap-3 items-center\">\n   166→          <div className=\"flex-1 relative\">\n   167→            <input\n   168→              type=\"text\"\n   169→              placeholder=\"Search by book title or author...\"\n   170→              value={searchQuery}\n   171→              onChange={(e) => handleSearchChange(e.target.value)}\n   172→              className=\"input-field\"\n   173→              style={{ paddingLeft: \"2.5rem\" }}\n   174→            />\n   175→            <svg\n   176→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   177→              style={{\n   178→                position: \"absolute\",\n   179→                left: \"0.75rem\",\n   180→                top: \"50%\",\n   181→                transform: \"translateY(-50%)\",\n   182→                color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   183→                width: \"1.125rem\",\n   184→                height: \"1.125rem\",\n   185→                pointerEvents: \"none\",\n   186→              }}\n   187→              fill=\"none\"\n   188→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   189→              strokeWidth={2}\n   190→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   191→            >\n   192→              <circle cx={11} cy={11} r={8} />\n   193→              <line x1={21} y1={21} x2={16.65} y2={16.65} />\n   194→            </svg>\n   195→          </div>\n   196→          <button\n   197→            onClick={() => setFiltersOpen(!filtersOpen)}\n   198→            className=\"btn-secondary\"\n   199→            aria-expanded={filtersOpen}\n   200→            aria-controls=\"filter-panel\"\n   201→            style={{\n   202→              display: \"flex\",\n   203→              alignItems: \"center\",\n   204→              gap: \"0.375rem\",\n   205→              whiteSpace: \"nowrap\",\n   206→              position: \"relative\",\n   207→            }}\n   208→          >\n   209→            <svg\n   210→              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   211→              width=\"16\"\n   212→              height=\"16\"\n   213→              viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   214→              fill=\"none\"\n   215→              stroke=\"currentColor\"\n   216→              strokeWidth={2}\n   217→            >\n   218→              <line x1={4} y1={6} x2={20} y2={6} />\n   219→              <line x1={7} y1={12} x2={17} y2={12} />\n   220→              <line x1={10} y1={18} x2={14} y2={18} />\n   221→            </svg>\n   222→            Filters\n   223→            {hasActiveFilters && (\n   224→              <span\n   225→                aria-hidden=\"true\"\n   226→                style={{\n   227→                  width: \"0.5rem\",\n   228→                  height: \"0.5rem\",\n   229→                  borderRadius: \"50%\",\n   230→                  backgroundColor: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n   231→                  position: \"absolute\",\n   232→                  top: \"0.375rem\",\n   233→                  right: \"0.375rem\",\n   234→                }}\n   235→              />\n   236→            )}\n   237→          </button>\n   238→        </div>\n   239→\n   240→        {/* Filter panel */}\n   241→        {filtersOpen && (\n   242→          <div\n   243→            id=\"filter-panel\"\n   244→            className=\"card mt-3\"\n   245→            style={{ padding: \"1rem 1.25rem\" }}\n   246→          >\n   247→            <div\n   248→              className=\"grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-3 sm:gap-4 items-end\"\n   249→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   250→            >\n   251→              <div>\n   252→                <label\n   253→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   254→                  style={{\n   255→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   256→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   257→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   258→                  }}\n   259→                >\n   260→                  Meeting format\n   261→                </label>\n   262→                <select\n   263→                  value={meetingFormat}\n   264→                  onChange={(e) => handleFormatChange(e.target.value)}\n   265→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   266→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   267→                >\n   268→                  <option value=\"\">All formats</option>\n   269→                  <option value=\"voice\">Voice calls</option>\n   270→                  <option value=\"text\">Text chat</option>\n   271→                  <option value=\"mixed\">Mixed</option>\n   272→                </select>\n   273→              </div>\n   274→\n   275→              <div>\n   276→                <label\n   277→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   278→                  style={{\n   279→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   280→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   281→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   282→                  }}\n   283→                >\n   284→                  Reading pace\n   285→                </label>\n   286→                <select\n   287→                  value={readingPace}\n   288→                  onChange={(e) => handlePaceChange(e.target.value)}\n   289→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   290→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   291→                >\n   292→                  <option value=\"\">All paces</option>\n   293→                  <option value=\"chapter\">Chapter-based</option>\n   294→                  <option value=\"pages\">Pages-based</option>\n   295→                  <option value=\"week\">Weekly</option>\n   296→                  <option value=\"day\">Daily</option>\n   297→                </select>\n   298→              </div>\n   299→\n   300→              <div>\n   301→                <label\n   302→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   303→                  style={{\n   304→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   305→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   306→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   307→                  }}\n   308→                >\n   309→                  Starting from\n   310→                </label>\n   311→                <input\n   312→                  type=\"date\"\n   313→                  value={startDateFrom}\n   314→                  onChange={(e) => handleStartDateFromChange(e.target.value)}\n   315→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   316→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   317→                />\n   318→              </div>\n   319→\n   320→              <div>\n   321→                <label\n   322→                  className=\"text-xs font-semibold mb-1 block\"\n   323→                  style={{\n   324→                    color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   325→                    textTransform: \"uppercase\",\n   326→                    letterSpacing: \"0.05em\",\n   327→                  }}\n   328→                >\n   329→                  Sort by\n   330→                </label>\n   331→                <select\n   332→                  value={sort}\n   333→                  onChange={(e) => handleSortChange(e.target.value)}\n   334→                  className=\"input-field\"\n   335→                  style={{ fontSize: \"0.875rem\", padding: \"0.5rem 0.75rem\" }}\n   336→                >\n   337→                  <option value=\"newest\">Newest first</option>\n   338→                  <option value=\"oldest\">Oldest first</option>\n   339→                  <option value=\"start_date\">Start date (soonest)</option>\n   340→                </select>\n   341→              </div>\n   342→\n   343→              {hasActiveFilters && (\n   344→                <button\n   345→                  onClick={clearFilters}\n   346→                  style={{\n   347→                    color: \"var(--color-accent)\",\n   348→                    fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   349→                    fontSize: \"0.875rem\",\n   350→                    fontWeight: 600,\n   351→                    cursor: \"pointer\",\n   352→                    background: \"none\",\n   353→                    border: \"none\",\n   354→                    padding: \"0.5rem 0\",\n   355→                  }}\n   356→                >\n   357→                  Clear all\n   358→                </button>\n   359→              )}\n   360→            </div>\n   361→          </div>\n   362→        )}\n   363→      </div>\n   364→\n   365→      {/* Results */}\n   366→      {loading ? (\n   367→        <div className=\"grid gap-4\">\n   368→          {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => (\n   369→            <div key={i} className=\"card flex gap-4\">\n   370→              <div\n   371→                className=\"skeleton\"\n   372→                style={{ width: \"4rem\", height: \"6rem\", flexShrink: 0 }}\n   373→              />\n   374→              <div className=\"flex-1\">\n   375→                <div\n   376→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\"\n   377→                  style={{ width: `${60 - i * 5}%` }}\n   378→                />\n   379→                <div\n   380→                  className=\"skeleton skeleton-text\"\n   381→                  style={{ width: `${45 - i * 5}%` }}\n   382→                />\n   383→                <div className=\"flex gap-2 mt-2\">\n   384→                  <div\n   385→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   386→                    style={{\n   387→                      width: \"5rem\",\n   388→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   389→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   390→                    }}\n   391→                  />\n   392→                  <div\n   393→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   394→                    style={{\n   395→                      width: \"4rem\",\n   396→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   397→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   398→                    }}\n   399→                  />\n   400→                  <div\n   401→                    className=\"skeleton\"\n   402→                    style={{\n   403→                      width: \"5.5rem\",\n   404→                      height: \"1.5rem\",\n   405→                      borderRadius: \"9999px\",\n   406→                    }}\n   407→                  />\n   408→                </div>\n   409→              </div>\n   410→            </div>\n   411→          ))}\n   412→        </div>\n   413→      ) : error ? (\n   414→        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   415→          <svg\n   416→            width=\"40\"\n   417→            height=\"40\"\n   418→            viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\"\n   419→            fill=\"none\"\n   420→            stroke=\"var(--color-error)\"\n   421→            strokeWidth={1.5}\n   422→            style={{ margin: \"0 auto 0.75rem\" }}\n   423→          >\n   424→            <circle cx={12} cy={12} r={10} />\n   425→            <line x1={12} y1={8} x2={12} y2={12} />\n   426→            <line x1={12} y1={16} x2={12.01} y2={16} />\n   427→          </svg>\n   428→          <p\n   429→            className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n   430→            style={{ color: \"var(--color-text)\", fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   431→          >\n   432→            {error}\n   433→          </p>\n   434→          <button\n   435→            onClick={() => fetchListings(searchQuery, meetingFormat, sort, readingPace, startDateFrom)}\n   436→            className=\"btn-primary\"\n   437→            style={{ marginTop: \"0.75rem\" }}\n   438→          >\n   439→            Try again\n   440→          </button>\n   441→        </div>\n   442→      ) : listings.length === 0 ? (\n   443→        <div className=\"text-center py-16\">\n   444→          {hasActiveFilters ? (\n   445→            <>\n   446→              <p\n   447→                className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n   448→                style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}\n   449→              >\n   450→                No matching reading groups\n   451→              </p>\n   452→              <p\n   453→                className=\"mb-6\"\n   454→                style={{\n   455→                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   456→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   457→                  fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n   458→                }}\n   459→              >\n   460→                Try adjusting your search or filters to find more groups.\n   461→              </p>\n   462→              <button onClick={clearFilters} className=\"btn-secondary\">\n   463→                Clear filters\n   464→              </button>\n   465→            </>\n   466→          ) : (\n   467→            <>\n   468→              <p\n   469→                className=\"text-lg mb-2\"\n   470→                style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\" }}\n   471→              >\n   472→                No reading groups yet\n   473→              </p>\n   474→              <p\n   475→                className=\"mb-6\"\n   476→                style={{\n   477→                  color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   478→                  fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   479→                  fontSize: \"0.95rem\",\n   480→                }}\n   481→              >\n   482→                Be the first to create a reading group and find your book\n   483→                companions.\n   484→              </p>\n   485→              <Link href=\"/listings/create\" className=\"btn-primary\">\n   486→                Create the first listing\n   487→              </Link>\n   488→            </>\n   489→          )}\n   490→        </div>\n   491→      ) : (\n   492→        <>\n   493→          <p\n   494→            className=\"mb-3 text-sm\"\n   495→            style={{\n   496→              color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\",\n   497→              fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\",\n   498→ ` · Page ${page} of ${totalPages}` : \"\"}\n   503→          </p>\n   504→          <div className=\"grid gap-4\" role=\"list\" aria-label=\"Reading groups\">\n   505→            {listings.map((listing) => (\n   506→              <Link\n   507→                key={listing.id}\n   508→                href={`/listings/${listing.id}`}\n   509→                className=\"card hover:shadow-md transition-shadow flex gap-4\"\n   510→                role=\"listitem\"\n   511→              >\n   512→                <BookCover\n   513→ */}\n   617→          {totalPages > 1 && (\n   618→            <nav\n   619→              className=\"flex justify-center items-center gap-2 mt-8\"\n   620→              aria-label=\"Pagination\"\n   621→              style={{ fontFamily: \"system-ui, sans-serif\" }}\n   622→            >\n   623→              <button\n   624→                onClick={() => handlePageChange(page - 1)}\n   625→                disabled={page <= 1}\n   626→                className=\"btn-secondary\"\n   627→                style={{\n   628→                  fontSize: \"0.875rem\",\n   629→                  padding: \"0.5rem 1rem\",\n   630→                  opacity: page <= 1 ? 0.5 : 1,\n   631→                  cursor: page <= 1 ? \"not-allowed\" : \"pointer\",\n   632→                }}\n   633→                aria-label=\"Previous page\"\n   634→              >\n   635→                Previous\n   636→              </button>\n   637→              <span\n   638→                className=\"text-sm\"\n   639→                style={{ color: \"var(--color-text-secondary)\", padding: \"0 0.5rem\" }}\n   640→              >\n   641→                {page} / {totalPages}\n   642→              </span>\n   643→
